Name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
User_Review:
It’s my 2nd time visiting Sigiriya after 12 years. Still it is gorgeous and the forest around Sigiriya is really beautiful. I can say Sri Lanka is a beautiful land after I visit Sigiriya.
As a foreigner, we should pay $55 for adults and free for kids. There are some shops you can find beverages and suveniours like magnets at the exit. I recommend to visit Sigiriya museum before you go to Sigiriya as it‘s at the ticket office building (entrance fee is included in your ticket).

Name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
User_Review:
Sigiriya is one of the most fascinating and breathtaking historical sites in Sri Lanka. Climbing the ancient rock fortress is an unforgettable experience — every step reveals something amazing, from the beautiful water gardens and the impressive lion’s paw entrance to the world-famous Sigiriya frescoes painted centuries ago.

The view from the top is absolutely stunning, offering a 360° panorama of lush forests and villages below. You can really feel the history and brilliance of King Kashyapa’s era in the design and architecture of the place.

It’s best to visit early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat. Make sure to wear comfortable shoes, bring water, and take your time exploring the site.

Overall, Sigiriya is a must-visit destination in Sri Lanka — a perfect combination of history, art, and natural beauty that leaves every visitor amazed.

Name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
User_Review: Entrance fee for foreigner is 35 USD but cheaper when you pay with rupee (guess they use worst exchange rate). The climb is about 1,000 steps - some steep. View from above is good and you can see all the foundation of old palace. Some old rock painting. Will be very crowned during the daytime so go 5 am! Sunrise is waiting for you!

Name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
User_Review: Sigiriya is a highly valuable archaeological site that is not religious in nature. One little-known fact is that its most significant part is not the top of the rock, but the lower section. Even UNESCO has designated it as the "Ancient City of Sigiriya." What I observe is that many people overlook the most important areas of Sigiriya and simply climb to the top of the rock. However, no king ever lived on top of the Sigiriya rock; instead, it was used more like a rooftop — mainly for the king’s leisure and cultural activities.

Name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
User_Review: Magnificent place to visit. Tremendous views from the top. $35 per foreign adult. The hiking itself might be challenging if you not in shape. However we are in our 40s and slightly above average and did it from ticket office to the top in 30 minutes. Whole journey with stops for guide explanation 1h30min. Ask for legit guide by the entrance or deal with cheeky local as we did. He got $10 and 1000 rupee and was happy. Best time to start before 10am to avoid crowds.

Name: Dambulla Cave Temple
User_Review: This is an active place of worship for Buddhist, so be considerate. The temple is under the UNESCO protection. The parking area for accessing the temple is not very obvious to visitors who are coming without a group. You have to buy a ticket at the parking lot and then climb about 200 to 300 stairs to access the cave on top of the mountain. You have to remove your shoes to access the temple. There are monkeys in the area. I have not seen a toilet near the temple, but there is one on the parking lot.

Name: Dambulla Cave Temple
User_Review:
Dambulla Cave Temple — also known as the Golden Temple of Dambulla — is one of Sri Lanka’s most magnificent and best-preserved cave temple complexes. Located in the Central Province of Sri Lanka, about 70 km north of Kandy, this UNESCO World Heritage Site dates back to the 1st century BCE. The temple complex consists of five main caves, each filled with ancient statues and vibrant murals depicting the life of the Buddha and various historical events.

Over 150 Buddha statues, along with statues of Sri Lankan kings and Hindu deities like Vishnu and Ganesh.

Beautiful murals covering over 2,000 square meters of cave walls and ceilings.

The Golden Temple with its massive golden Buddha statue at the base of the rock.

Stunning panoramic views of the surrounding countryside from the top of the hill.

Name: Dambulla Cave Temple
User_Review:
is an impressive spot with a lot of history and religious importance. Be prepared for a bit of a climb — over 300 steps lead you to the top — and don’t forget that modest clothing is required (no bare shoulders, and legs should be covered below the knees).

At the top, you’ll find great views and caves filled with statues and centuries-old paintings that are still in good condition. The site is mainly dedicated to Buddhist figures, though you’ll notice some Hindu statues as well. The huge golden Buddha at the base is also quite a sight.

The only downside is that the caves have no real ventilation, so the air can feel heavy, especially when it’s crowded.

Overall, it’s worth seeing once for the cultural and spiritual value — especially if you’re into temples — but probably not a place you’d return to again and again.

In August 2025 the price is 3000 rupees

Name: Dambulla Cave Temple
User_Review: Great place to visit, very interesting and beautiful temple. Nice views from the top. Long trousers and sleeves needed. 1500 rupees per foreign adult. If u arrive from the gold Buddha and stupa side you will need hike over the hill to ticket office (uncool when 35°C outside).

Name: Dambulla Cave Temple
User_Review:
The Dambulla Royal Cave Temple and Golden Temple is a fascinating historic and religious site worth visiting. Be prepared for a climb — there are over 300 steps to reach the top, and it can get busy with tourists, so an early visit is ideal. As this is a sacred Buddhist site, a modest dress code is enforced: legs must be covered below the knee, and no bare shoulders are allowed.

Once you reach the top, you’re rewarded with beautiful panoramic views of the surrounding area. Inside the caves, the statues and wall paintings are remarkably well preserved, showcasing centuries of artistry and devotion. While the majority of the site is dedicated to Buddhist figures, you will also spot a few statues of Hindu gods, adding a unique cultural touch. The Golden Temple at the base is equally striking, with its massive seated Buddha statue welcoming visitors.

Overall, it’s a place of spiritual significance, rich history, and stunning craftsmanship — a must-see when in the region.

Name: Temple of the Tooth Relic
User_Review: The Dalada Maligawa is truly a sacred and peaceful place filled with history and spiritual beauty. The atmosphere is calm and respectful, and the architecture is breathtaking. A must-visit in Kandy to experience Sri Lanka’s deep culture and devotion. Absolutely five stars! 🌸🙏

Name: Temple of the Tooth Relic
User_Review: The Buddha Tooth Relic Temple (Sri Dalada Maligawa) in Kandy is a spectacular and deeply spiritual site with great historical importance. The temple houses the revered tooth relic of Lord Buddha, attracting pilgrims and travelers from around the world. The stunning Kandyan architecture, intricate carvings, and peaceful lake surroundings make every visit truly memorable. Rituals and ceremonies held here offer a unique glimpse into Sri Lanka’s rich Buddhist traditions. The temple’s serene ambience and welcoming atmosphere create an uplifting experience for all who visit—an absolute highlight of any trip to Kandy

Name: Temple of the Tooth Relic
User_Review:
Visiting Sri Dalada Maligawa was a profoundly moving experience — this isn’t just a temple, it’s Sri Lanka’s beating spiritual heart. The Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic in Kandy holds more than the tooth of the Buddha; it holds centuries of history, tradition, and devotion that still ripple through every ceremony and corridor.

From the moment you walk past the majestic Makara Thorana gateway, cross the moat, through the “Walakulu Bamma” (Cloud Wall), you can almost feel the weight of kings who built, preserved, destroyed, and rebuilt this site to protect the relic. The architecture — Kandyan style intricate wood and ivory carving, golden canopies, the sacred chamber that holds the relic in nested golden caskets — is breathtaking.

Name: Temple of the Tooth Relic
User_Review: Place has much more local relevance if you deep dive into history. Great connection with India and how the tooth was brought. Extremely clean and do explore the museums inside because most people skip that. It’s a bit pricey for the location but I guess it all goes into maintenance so worth it.

Name: Temple of the Tooth Relic
User_Review:
A lovely temple for the Buddhists that is extremely sacred because of the tooth that was recovered from the pure of lord Buddha. That tooth has been preserved here and is worshipped as a part of the lord himself. The tooth bears huge significance for being equivalent to Lord Buddha or as the last remains of him.

You must leave your shoes and enter the temple with flowers that can be offered inside. There are two floors, ground and first wherein the chanting and prayer happens.

If you get time, you must sit and take in the full experience. There's a museum as well to visit. I would recommend this place to everyone visiting Kandy.

Name: Peradeniya Botanical Garden
User_Review:
The Royal Botanical Garden in Kandy is one of the most beautiful and peaceful places I have ever visited. The moment you walk in you feel surrounded by nature in its purest form. The variety of trees, flowers and plants is incredible and you can spend hours just wandering through the paths without getting bored. The orchid house is stunning and the collection of palm trees creates such a unique atmosphere. I really enjoyed the wide open lawns where you can sit down and take in the scenery while watching families, couples and travelers enjoying the same calmness. The garden is very well maintained and it feels like every corner has something different to discover whether it is giant bamboos, bright tropical flowers or ancient trees with roots spreading like art across the ground.

It is also a great place to escape the busy city for a few hours. The air feels fresher here and the whole environment is relaxing and refreshing. If you love photography or simply want to connect with nature this is a perfect spot. I would highly recommend visiting the Royal Botanical Garden if you are in Kandy. It is not just a tourist attraction but truly a natural treasure that leaves you feeling inspired and peaceful.

Name: Peradeniya Botanical Garden
User_Review:
A Refreshing Escape at Peradeniya Royal Botanical Gardens

Visiting the Royal Botanical Gardens in Peradeniya was an absolute delight. The lush greenery, vibrant flowers, and beautifully maintained landscapes make it a perfect spot to unwind and reconnect with nature.

From towering palm avenues to exotic orchids and serene lakes, every corner offers something breathtaking. The gardens are well-organized, clean, and ideal for photography, picnics, or a peaceful walk.

Highly recommend this gem to anyone visiting Kandy, it’s a must-see for nature lovers and explorers alike.. Rating: 10/10

Name: Peradeniya Botanical Garden
User_Review:
The Royal Botanic Gardens in Peradeniya are absolutely stunning and exceeded all expectations. The grounds are vast, beautifully landscaped, and immaculately maintained. You can spend hours walking through the different sections – from the impressive orchid collection, to the towering palm avenues, giant bamboo groves, and massive century-old trees.

The atmosphere is peaceful and refreshing, making it the perfect escape from the busy city. It’s also a paradise for photography and nature lovers. Every corner feels unique and full of beauty.

This was honestly one of the best experiences in Sri Lanka – highly recommended for anyone visiting Kandy.

Name: Peradeniya Botanical Garden
User_Review:
This garden or gardens as the name suggests spans over 146 acres. It's massive and for my injured foot this was a huge task to complete. But I'm proud that I finished half of the garden on my first visit.

The entire area is full of trees and birds and monkeys. Ever feel like you need some fresh air, well you'll get a lot of it here. Beautiful scenes, peaceful and clean. The green just gives a sense of immense calmness alimg with the variety of flowers.

One must visit this if nearby or if planning to come to Peradeniya.

Name: Peradeniya Botanical Garden
User_Review: Lovely place to visit and calm inside the chaos of the city. Its around £10 entrance fee, take water although there is a small cafe at the entrance and a local small canteen inside the park. You can hire a golf cart if you like which is good for people less able to walk around the huge park.

Name: Kandy Lake
User_Review:
"Beautiful Viewpoint in Kandy"
I recently visited this viewpoint in Kandy, and the scenery was amazing. You can see the whole city with the mountains and greenery all around. It’s a peaceful place to relax, take photos, and enjoy the fresh air. Sometimes it can be a little bit busy here, but it doesn’t disturb the experience. Sunset time is especially nice when the city slowly lights up. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in Kandy.
🍃✨️

Name: Kandy Lake
User_Review: A great spot above Kandy offering beautiful panoramic views of the city — especially stunning at sunset or after dark. There are a few souvenir sellers around, but they’re not too pushy if you politely decline. During our visit, it wasn’t too crowded, which made the experience even more enjoyable. A peaceful and scenic place that's well worth a short stop while exploring Kandy.

Name: Kandy Lake
User_Review: Awesome place to visit family and friends to see the beauty of the whole country city. It's look like a lighting show in the evening. Literally we can see the mesmerizing view of the reflection Hindi Kandy lake

Name: Kandy Lake
User_Review: Love this place to see the whole picture of 소 city of Kandy. Good experience to see and take pictures. You can see the lake and the temple at one glance and see the other parts of the city.

Name: Kandy Lake
User_Review:
My wife, Shirleen, our child, and I recently visited the famous Kandy View Point—also known as Arthur’s Seat—just a short hike from the Temple of the Tooth. Perched 550 m above the city, it offers sweeping views over Kandy Lake, the Temple of the Tooth Relic, the Royal Palace, and the surrounding hills.

The panorama was breathtaking. We arrived mid‑afternoon, but locals and fellow tourists recommend early morning or late afternoon for the best light and cooler temperatures . As we soaked in the view, birds chirped, monkeys swung through the trees, and a gentle breeze kept us refreshed. The platform is not that much spacious but well‑laid out—perfect for family photos.

Local Vendors

Scattered near the viewpoint are small vendors selling souvenirs and snacks. While culturally intriguing, they tend to price their items quite high—many charging several times more than city shops. We politely declined their offers, choosing instead to enjoy the view and capture memories with our camera.

Name: Knuckles Mountain Range
User_Review: We had a fantastic time with Hema the guide and his porter Raja. The 1st day was tough but so rewarding, going through various terrains, waterfalls and streams. We stopped just under the main Knuckles Mountain and had an amazing Sri Lankan lunch, lovingly prepared by Hema's wife - so delicious, it was worth the trek on it's own!!. It was raining on our second day but the walk through tea planations, rice fields and hillside villages was beautiful. We would highly recommend Hema and if you have any concerns, just email him and he will answer them.

Name: Knuckles Mountain Range
User_Review: I have enjoyed this trip all through out. The first day hike was a bit too much but Hema assisted and adjusted our plans to accommodate me. The food was always good and the accommodations were all unique experiences. Hema has also introduced us to the Sri Lankan culture which was really nice as he’s shared some things and activities with us which you can only learn with a local Sri Lankan. He’s also very knowledgeable about the plants and animals which made the hikes more fun as we go through it learning new stuff and species of just about everything.

Name: Knuckles Mountain Range
User_Review:
Absolutely fantastic hike today with Hemantha and Mr Seva! We saw some breathtaking views along the way and learned lots of interesting facts about the local flora and wildlife from Hema. The trek was challenging but rewarding and we really could not have done it without their knowledge of the trails.

We were treated to a delicious Sri Lankan lunch on the mountain which was homemade by Hema’s wife and the day was topped off with a lovely cup of tea in a small Sri Lankan village near Rangala.

If you are in Kandy and are a keen hiker then Hemantha is your man!

Name: Knuckles Mountain Range
User_Review:
Really great experience trekking in the Knuckles Mountain Range. We did option 1 2d/1n as we got into Kandy the morning of and Hema picked us up from the train station. As mentioned by others, the first day is definitely the hardest but not impossible. For stats nerds like us it was 12km and 600m+ (less than the expected 18km). The views alone made it worth it though. Day 2 was leisurely and entirely downhill through tea plantations and a valley, about 8km. Hema was with us for most of the first day but not the second day which was a surprise. Raja was with us the entire time and he was very kind and considerate. Both pointed out various flora and fauna along the hike.

The food was an absolute highlight of this entire trip. Lunch made by Hema’s wife, dinner and breakfast made by the lovely ladies at the homestay, and a stop for lunch on the way back to Kandy was good too.

The trip lost a star from the homestay accommodation. The room was basic and comfy. However, there were a lot of mosquitos and there was no fan or mosquito net in the room to help keep them away. This also meant we couldn’t have the windows open so the room was hot and stuffy. Luckily, the second day of the trek was really easy because we were quite tired.

To be fair, we did not tell Hema about this problem (it was the middle of the night) so he didn’t have a chance to fix it.

Overall, a few unexpected parts and perhaps just an unlucky mosquito night made this a 4 instead of a 5. Would still recommend to friends!

Name: Knuckles Mountain Range
User_Review: Did the 22km trek (Option 3 day hike) up the Knuckles highest peak. Had an amazing day. The walk was brilliant - amazing views all day, and through tea plantations, forests and to a waterfall. Hema made us a brilliant veggie rice and curry for lunch which we ate part way up the mountain. Picked us up and dropped us to our hotel. Would really recommend.

Name: Horton Plains National Park
User_Review:
Horton Plains National Park is one of the main national parks located in the Central Highlands of Sri Lanka. It’s home to a rich variety of flora, fauna, and bird species, making it a great place for nature lovers and photographers.

The natural scenery is breathtaking, with misty mountains, grasslands, and forests. Tickets are available for both local and foreign visitors.

There are no camping areas, as this is a sensitive natural environment, so visitors are encouraged to respect and protect its beauty.
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Name: Horton Plains National Park
User_Review:
“An unforgettable experience! Horton Plains is one of the most beautiful and well-preserved nature reserves in Sri Lanka. From the moment you enter, you're surrounded by cool mountain air, peaceful silence, and stunning scenery. The 9.5 km circular trail is perfect for nature lovers and offers a mix of grasslands, cloud forests, and waterfalls. The highlight, of course, is World’s End — a dramatic cliff with panoramic views that will take your breath away. On clear mornings, you can see all the way to the south coast.

Baker’s Falls is another gem along the trail, with refreshing mist and a picture-perfect view. You’ll likely spot wildlife too — like sambar deer, birds, and unique plants found nowhere else. The path is well-marked and not too difficult, but wear good shoes and bring water. It’s also important to go early to avoid the crowds and get the clearest views.

Truly a magical place that combines adventure, peace, and natural beauty in one journey. Highly recommended for hikers, photographers, and anyone wanting to connect with nature!” 🌿🏞️📸

Name: Horton Plains National Park
User_Review:
Horton Plains is such a peaceful and refreshing place. The cool weather, misty views, World’s End, and Baker’s Falls make it unforgettable. A perfect spot to enjoy nature in the highlands.

🎫 Ticket Price reasonable. (Adults 150/= Children 100/=
⏳ Open daily from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM, but entry is usually allowed only up to 2:00 PM since visitors must leave before closing.
🛣️ Approximately  10 km trail to round trip

Name: Horton Plains National Park
User_Review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Horton Plains National Park – A Breathtaking Wonderland in the Clouds 🌿☁️

Horton Plains is one of the most beautiful and unique places in Sri Lanka — a true paradise for nature lovers and hikers. The cool misty weather, endless grasslands, and stunning mountain views make it an unforgettable experience.

The main hiking trail is about 9 km and takes you through forests, open plains, and viewpoints that are absolutely worth the effort. The highlight, World’s End, offers a jaw-dropping panoramic view — especially early in the morning before the mist rolls in. Baker’s Falls is another must-see spot along the trail, with refreshing air and the sound of cascading water. 💧🌳

The park is also home to rare wildlife, including sambar deer and various bird species. It’s clean, well-managed, and the atmosphere feels pure and untouched.

💬 Highlights:
•	Breathtaking scenery and hiking trails
•	World’s End & Baker’s Falls viewpoints
•	Cool, misty climate
•	Great spot for photography and nature walks

If you visit Nuwara Eliya, Horton Plains is a must-visit destination — peaceful, wild, and truly awe-inspiring. 🌄❤️

Name: Horton Plains National Park
User_Review: The Horton Plains Nelu flower season is truly a magical time in Sri Lanka. Once in several years, the plains come alive with a sea of delicate purples and blues, covering the misty grasslands in an unforgettable bloom. Surrounded by cool winds, cloud-kissed peaks, and untouched wilderness, walking through the Nelu-covered trails feels like stepping into a living painting. It’s a rare natural event that touches the heart and soul — a quiet reminder of nature’s hidden wonders waiting to be discovered.

Name: Gregory Lake
User_Review: One of the most beautiful lakes that I have seen. We enjoyed noating here. The water is blue and the surrounding have a colonial charm. The adjoining garden is meticulously maintained. The place have plenty of boating options. After all the fun you may stop at lakeside shops for tea and snacks. The sunset was so beautiful.

Name: Gregory Lake
User_Review: Gregory Lake is a beautiful place in Nuwara Eliya, but it also has a not-so-good side. The small shops nearby sell food at very high prices, and most of them aren’t very hygienic, so it’s better to avoid eating there. Also, visiting during the peak season isn’t recommended because the traffic gets really heavy, and even the boat rides are quite expensive.

Name: Gregory Lake
User_Review:
Lake Gregory is one of the most beautiful and refreshing places to visit in Nuwara Eliya. The cool climate, misty surroundings, and stunning views of the water create the perfect atmosphere for relaxation and sightseeing. The area is clean, well maintained, and ideal for both families and couples.
There’s plenty to do as well—boat rides, pony rides, and even the option to rent a bicycle and cycle around the lake. The walking paths and open spaces make it perfect for a peaceful stroll or a picnic by the water. With food stalls and seating areas nearby, it’s a place where you can easily spend a few hours just unwinding. Lake Gregory is truly a highlight of the hill country and an experience I highly recommend

Name: Gregory Lake
User_Review:
Lake Gregory is a beautiful and lively place to visit with your loved ones. It can get very busy, so make sure to buy your entry ticket in advance. Activities like boat rides and horse/pony rides are available, though prices are on the higher side.
Parking is available, but a ticket is required. Nearby shops sell toys, scarves, jackets, and ice caps, but they are also quite expensive. The lake area is very scenic and perfect for photos, with many hotels located close by.

Name: Gregory Lake
User_Review:
We had an amazing time at Lake Gregory. The Place was so peaceful with stunning views of the lake surrounded by misty mountains. The boat rides, jet ski and bicycle ride experience were thrilling and gave us a different way to enjoy the lake. The cool climate made the whole visit refreshing, and it’s a perfect spot to relax and breathe in nature.

There are plenty of nice photo spots around the lake ideal for capturing memories with friends and family. Highly recommend this place if you love nature, adventure, and beautiful scenery. A must-visit when you’re in Nuwara Eliya.

Name: Ramboda Falls
User_Review:
The sound of the rushing water and the cool breeze make it such a peaceful and refreshing place to visit.

The waterfall itself is tall, powerful, and picture-perfect — a must-see spot for anyone traveling through Sri Lanka’s hill country.

If you love nature, this place will leave you speechless. Don’t forget your camera — every angle is stunning! 🌊

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ — Highly recommended for anyone who wants to experience the beauty of Sri Lanka!

Name: Ramboda Falls
User_Review: Visited Ramboda Waterfall recently, and it was absolutely amazing. The view was breathtaking, with water cascading down the rocks surrounded by lush greenery. The atmosphere was peaceful and refreshing—perfect for a short escape into nature. The cool mist from the falls added to the experience, making it feel truly magical. It’s a must-visit spot if you’re exploring Sri Lanka’s hill country. I highly recommend it!

Name: Ramboda Falls
User_Review: It was raining when I visited it and I was alone there. Super waterfall and great photo opportunity. There was more water falling due to the rain and it was super cool and mighty. Entrance is 200rupee and it is worth it. The climb is easy even with regular sneakers. After seeing this waterfall I skipped all the rest that were on my itinerary.

Name: Ramboda Falls
User_Review:
Our Tuktuk driver recommended us visiting this waterfall instead of Lovers Leap and in hindsight, I couldn’t agree more with him.

We had to pay 200 LKR for entry, but it’s more than worth that. The hike is advertised as easy but I would recommend people bring water and put on hiking shoes. Also bring long socks so the leeches don’t get you.

You can swim which we didn’t because it was too cold, but it looks lovely.

Name: Ramboda Falls
User_Review:
I recently visited Ramboda Falls and it was absolutely breathtaking! The waterfall is tall, powerful, and surrounded by lush greenery that makes the whole experience feel magical. There’s a nice viewpoint where you can take amazing photos, and the sound of the water is so calming.

The area was clean and not too crowded when I went. Make sure to wear comfortable shoes if you plan to hike a bit closer to the falls. Highly recommend stopping here if you’re exploring the Nuwara Eliya area!

Name: Galle Fort
User_Review: Galle Dutch Fort is an incredible place to visit — full of history, charm, and beautiful views. Walking along the ramparts at sunset is a highlight, with stunning scenery over the ocean on one side and the old town on the other. Inside the fort, the narrow streets are filled with colonial buildings, cafes, shops, and museums, all blending history with a relaxed coastal vibe. It’s well-preserved, easy to explore on foot, and has something for everyone, whether you’re into history, photography, or just enjoying the atmosphere. A must-visit when in Sri Lanka.

Name: Galle Fort
User_Review:
We had such a wonderful time exploring the Galle Dutch Fort as a couple with our 10-year-old. It’s such a charming place — full of history, character, and amazing ocean views.

We visited the lighthouse, clock tower, and walked along the rampart walls, catching the sunset from Flag Rock Bastion Point — absolutely stunning! The golden light over the ocean made it feel magical.

We also enjoyed a bit of shopping at Spa Ceylon and Barefoot, wandered through the quaint alleys, and treated ourselves to some ice cream before having dinner at one of the food courts near the entrance.

The blend of colonial architecture, relaxed vibe, and sea breeze made it a perfect family evening. Highly recommend spending a few hours here — it’s one of the most beautiful spots in Sri Lanka! 🌅🏰🌊

Name: Galle Fort
User_Review: Galle Fort is a breathtaking blend of history, culture, and coastal charm. The cobblestone streets, colonial architecture, and ocean views create an unforgettable atmosphere. From boutique cafes to art galleries, every corner tells a story. Perfect for a relaxing stroll, shopping, or sunset moments truly a gem of Sri Lanka!

Name: Galle Fort
User_Review: This attraction is free, but it is very well kept and visited my tourists. You cannot only see the remnants of the fortification, but also some wild birds nesting. There are a couple of nice Instagram worthy photo occasions. The information boards across the fort are not available sometimes due to the weather erosion. The inside of the Ford, which is populated with souvenir, shops and restaurants is not very crowded. You can expect to spend 2 to 3 hours wandering around the fort.

Name: Galle Fort
User_Review: Lovely views and a historical site. Lots of people both young and old, couples and families out relaxing and enjoying themselves. Beautiful photo opportunities and many restaurants around too. Many people just sat watching the world pass them by.

Name: Unawatuna Beach
User_Review:
Unawatuna Beach is one of the most beautiful beaches in Sri Lanka, clean golden sand, clear water, and a perfect place to relax or take a swim. The sunset views are absolutely stunning, and there are plenty of beach cafés and restaurants around to enjoy good food and drinks with a view.

It’s great for families, couples, and even solo travelers who just want to chill. The water is usually calm and safe for swimming, especially in the morning. Evenings can get a bit crowded, but the atmosphere is lively and fun.

Parking can be a little tricky during weekends, and it’s better to go early if you want a good spot near the beach. Overall, a must-visit beach for anyone traveling to the south coast peaceful, scenic, and full of good vibes! 🌊☀️

Name: Unawatuna Beach
User_Review: Unawatuna Beach is one of the most beautiful beaches in Sri Lanka, famous for its golden sand and calm blue water. It’s perfect for swimming, relaxing, and watching the sunset. There are plenty of restaurants and cafes nearby, making it a great place to spend the whole day enjoying the sea and the atmosphere.

Name: Unawatuna Beach
User_Review:
A lively and beautiful beach with golden sand and a vibrant atmosphere. There are plenty of cafes and bars offering sunbeds, towels, delicious seafood, and refreshing drinks. However, it’s a good idea to check in advance whether they have proper washroom and changing facilities.
Boat rides and other water activities are available. One of the best beaches in Sri Lanka!

Name: Unawatuna Beach
User_Review: This beach is so dirty... as anywhere else in Sri Lanka. If actions are not taken to manage garbage and educate local people, turists will stop visiting this beautiful country.

Name: Unawatuna Beach
User_Review:
Unawatuna Beach is truly a must-visit destination for both Sri Lankans and foreign travelers alike. The natural beauty of this beach is breathtaking — its distinct curved shoreline and gentle sea waves offer a relaxing, eye-pleasing atmosphere that immediately captures your attention.

We felt we could easily stay here for days without doing anything — it’s that visually pleasing and peaceful. Just being by the shore, listening to the waves, and taking in the view is more than enough to make time feel like it’s standing still. It’s the kind of place that naturally encourages you to slow down and simply enjoy the moment.

For those seeking a bit more activity, boat rides and other water-based experiences are available. These are typically offered by local boat operators, and prices are flexible based on direct negotiation. It’s best to discuss your preferences and agree on the details before heading out.

There’s a good mix of local vendors, eateries, and accommodations nearby, making it easy to plan a comfortable stay. Whether you’re looking for adventure or relaxation, Unawatuna delivers both in its own charming way.

Overall, this is undoubtedly one of the best beaches in Sri Lanka — highly recommended for families, couples, or solo travelers who want to experience nature, comfort, and coastal bliss all in one place.

Name: Mirissa Beach
User_Review: Had an amazing experience. We went 8 miles deep into the sea to watch whales. We also saw a few dolphins in the mid. Finally we saw blue whales for 4-5 times. The guides were very helpful in navigating the location of the whales and making sure everyone spots them. Seasickness was handled well and they make sure there is no lastic in the ocean.

Name: Mirissa Beach
User_Review:
*5-Star Whale Watching Experience in Sri Lanka*

I recently had the pleasure of going whale watching in Sri Lanka, and it was truly an unforgettable experience. The service provided by the tour operator was exceptional from start to finish. Their boat is not only the largest but also the fastest in the country, making the journey smooth, safe, and incredibly efficient.

What stood out the most was their outstanding hospitality. The crew was warm, friendly, and highly professional, ensuring every guest felt comfortable and well cared for. Their commitment to customer satisfaction is evident in every detail, from safety briefings to refreshments on board.

This kind of high-quality service not only makes the experience memorable but also significantly contributes to promoting tourism in Sri Lanka. I highly recommend this provider to anyone looking for a world-class whale watching adventure. They truly deserve a five-star rating!

Name: Mirissa Beach
User_Review: The staff are so lovely and know exactly where to go to see the whales. They keep a respectful distance so as to not disturb the whales but also allow to have a good view (ensuring both sides of the boat get a good look). They can easily tell you what type of whale you’re looking at and lots of information about them! They also provide snacks and a drink. An amazing experience! I would definitely recommend!!

Name: Mirissa Beach
User_Review:
I have visited in September 2025 with my friends, it was a good experience.
The ride was excellent, the crew is knowledgeable. They know what they are doing.
We have seen the dolphin, turtles and  blue whale.
The boat left sharp at 7 and we came back around 10.
Whoever is having Sea Sick ness this trip is not for them, because the boat shakes a lot. So avoid the same if you were cant handle the Sea motion sickness.
The trip is worth it.
I preferred to go Merissa by bus via Matara. Take a AC Express bus from Colombo, Petha bus stand it will drop you in 2.5 hrs to Matara from them in 20 minutes you can go to Merissa,
Enjoy the surfing and beach parties then in the morning whale watching. After that Hikkaduwa in the afternoon.
Then from Galle Railway station take a express train to colombo which will take 1.5 Hrs.
I am not saying this to save money , its for convenience and fast mode of transport.
Attached some Videos of the Whale and mirissa beach

Name: Mirissa Beach
User_Review: Amazing experience! We spotted 5 or 6 blue whales, all around us, and the crew were knowledgeable, professional, and truly respectful of the animals. A rare, ethical whale watching tour where the guides genuinely care about the environment and the whales. Highly recommended!

Name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef
User_Review: For coral reef boat ride there are scammers. Most of the corals are dead and some of them are planted artificially. difficult to find parking and snorkeling will be a good option instead of the boat ride.

Name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef
User_Review:
Hikkaduwa is an amazing place to enjoy, though weekends tend to get crowded. For the best experience, I recommend visiting during the season (November to March). There are plenty of activities to enjoy, such as snorkeling, diving, swimming, or taking a glass-bottom boat tour. Unfortunately, most of the corals in Hikkaduwa are dead, but you can still find some live ones. However, many locals (and sometimes even foreigners) don’t seem to care about the corals, and their actions often contribute to the destruction of the remaining live coral.

Despite this, there are still many fish and a few turtles to see. If you want a truly great experience, visit early in the morning before it gets crowded. Let’s all do our part to save nature! 🍂

Name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef
User_Review:
Excellent snorkeling with plenty of fish, even in the shallows. You can venture out a little further, past the Red bouys, and see some of the larger fish
We hired masks and fins - 1000 per set. We also rented Sun beds next to Hikka Joint Beer Garden. 3000 for beds next to each other.
Really enjoyed the beach and snorkeling!

Name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef
User_Review: Almost all dead corals in a beautiful beach. There are few sea creatures to be spotted. But seemingly, it is plummeting due to on going environmental pollution like as almost everywhere else. There are many hotels around here as a tourist attracted site. Also plenty of fishing boats are around, plus snorkeling and surfing facilities can be found.

Name: Hikkaduwa Coral Reef
User_Review:
Beautiful sandy kilometers long beach.Soft sand and the clean beach! Take a walk by the sea in the early morning hours, it's a perfect beginning of the day and best place for snorkeling,
Here i try to swim every damn day I can, and finally I've learned to snorkel.

Name: Yala National Park Safari Entry
User_Review: Great, we saw two leopards up close. In addition, the scenery in the forest was magnificent. We were also fascinated by crocodiles hiding in the water, wild buffaloes bathing in the mud, and peacocks dancing with their feathers. We saw elephants roaming in groups and elephants living alone. This full day safari was provided to us by Keshara Safari. Their lunch and breakfast foods was perfect. We also took advantage of their other services. I highly recommend this Yala National Park to everyone. Overall, it was Keshara Safari that gave us the chance to see leopards. We rented a bike from them and went to see the environment outside the Yala jungle. I highly recommend Yala National Park and Keshara Safari.

Name: Yala National Park Safari Entry
User_Review: Overall, the park is very beautiful. But for my taste, too many of the roads are placed directly through the jungle, so you cannot see very far. Good, if you are looking for all the smaller animals, like all sorts of birds, Mungos, Cormorans, Deers, Peacocks etc. We did see like 15 different species incl. a sleepy Leopard but no Elephants. But you can't be lucky all the time. 😉

Name: Yala National Park Safari Entry
User_Review:
A truly unique adventure and my very first safari! Safaris are always about luck since animals live freely and often hide from the noise of vehicles, but we got super lucky. We spotted plenty of monkeys, deer, crocodiles, peacocks, oxen, lots of birds, smaller animals, and even half of a leopard’s body 😂

If I get the chance to go on safari again, I’d definitely wear UV-protection sleeves, and I noticed some people wore masks because of the sand. Be prepared to get completely dusty and dirty from the drive, don’t plan on wearing those clothes again during your trip!

Name: Yala National Park Safari Entry
User_Review: Had a nice safari experience here. The driver kept us explaining about the parts, flora and fauna of the park. The park is endowed with elephants, leopards, birds and a wide varieties of other animals. Unluckily we weren't able to spot any leopard but we're lucky to enough to capture other animals and birds in the natural habitat.

Name: Yala National Park Safari Entry
User_Review: You either show up at 6am or 6pm, i went for the 6am, nice sunrise view. Didnt see elephant though, probably because it’s a dry day. You should bring something to cover your face from the dirt on the road. And only after i went that i learnt you should come after full moon, more likely to see the leopard. But anyway, good experience. Nice tourguide, he tried so hard

Name: Udawalawe National Park
User_Review:
Ticket prices are fair and reasonable.

A visit to Udawalawe National Park is one of the best safari experiences you can have in Sri Lanka. The park is open daily from 6.00 AM to 6.00 PM, giving travelers a full day’s opportunity to explore its rich wildlife and breathtaking scenery. Safaris here are well known for offering sightings of elephants, leopards, and many other wild animals in their natural habitats.

On our visit, we were especially fortunate to witness nearly 20 wild elephants, a truly spectacular sight that set the tone for the journey. The highlight of the day was watching a single majestic male elephant walking gracefully by the lakeside—a picture-perfect moment that will remain unforgettable. His calm yet powerful stride captured the essence of the wild in its purest form.

The safari ride itself remained exciting throughout, as there was always something to watch—whether it was herds of elephants, water buffalo, deer, crocodiles basking in the sun, or a variety of colorful birds. Each turn of the jeep revealed a new scene of natural beauty and wildlife interaction, keeping the experience fresh and thrilling.

For both locals and foreigners, Udawalawe is a must-visit destination. It combines adventure with awe, offering a rare chance to see animals in their true environment. With its abundance of elephants and diverse wildlife, it undoubtedly ranks as one of the top safari parks in Sri Lanka. Highly recommended for anyone visiting the island.

Name: Udawalawe National Park
User_Review:
I booked a tour from Mirissa to Udawalawe, and our driver picked us up right from the hotel for a comfortable two-hour drive to the park entrance, where we met our guide and transferred to the safari jeep.

The park entrance fee was very reasonable. Udawalawe National Park truly offers one of Sri Lanka’s best safari experiences, known for its close-up encounters with elephants, leopards, and a variety of wildlife in their natural habitats.

We were lucky to spot over 15 wild elephants, including a majestic male strolling gracefully by the lakeside — who even seemed slightly annoyed by our long stares! It was an unforgettable moment.

The safari stayed exciting from start to finish, with sightings of water buffalo, deer, crocodiles, foxes, iguanas, and vibrant tropical birds around every turn.

A visit to Udawalawe is an absolute must for both locals and tourists — the perfect blend of adventure, natural beauty, and abundant wildlife. Highly recommended.

Name: Udawalawe National Park
User_Review: Udawalawe National Park is one of Sri Lanka’s best wildlife destinations, especially for spotting wild elephants in their natural habitat. The park offers open landscapes with a scenic reservoir, making animal sightings easy and enjoyable. Jeep safaris give close views of elephants, buffalo, deer, crocodiles, and a wide variety of birds. Compared to other parks, Udawalawe is less crowded and provides a more relaxed safari experience. A visit to the nearby Elephant Transit Home adds extra value, where orphaned baby elephants are cared for before release into the wild. Overall, it’s a must-visit for nature lovers and wildlife photographers.

Name: Udawalawe National Park
User_Review:
Many elephants, birds of all kinds, water buffalo, field monitor(like a big lizzard), deers, animals of all kinds.
The plains where the elephants are gathering is super nice! Many safaris though.
Also some trash here and there :(

Name: Udawalawe National Park
User_Review: Incredible park! Lots of free elephants, some nice birds and a beautiful open space landscape. Only downside is the excessive agresivnes of the drivers and the amount of plastics near the water bodies

Name: Weligama Bay
User_Review: I honestly had one of the best weeks ever here at The Surfer Camp. The staff are all super friendly, attentive, and helpful, the food is really delicious, and every evening there are well-organized activities like karaoke, beer pong, or parties. If you seriously want to learn how to surf, you should definitely come here. The groups are very small, with a maximum of five people, so you can work closely with the surf instructor. The video analysis sessions every evening are great for spotting and improving your mistakes. They really try to bring out the best in you when it comes to surfing. I can only recommend it and would come back in a heartbeat.

Name: Weligama Bay
User_Review:
I decided to try surfing for the first time in Sri Lanka. It was important to me to find a place that was focused on the teaching and that was locally owned. The surfer was everything and more.

By sheer chance I managed to get promoted from beginner to the next level, and every instructor I had through out the 6 days I spent here was patient, educational and able to really help me understand the best way for me to improve.

By the end of my time I was able to catch angle the board and ride the wave all by myself! With some guidance from Sohan of course (who did an amazing job of keeping me calm when I felt frustrated)

A massive thank you to the whole team, especially Sohan, Nadiw, and Bunny for getting me to that point.

I’ll definitely be back!

Name: Weligama Bay
User_Review:
A Perfect Surf Retreat with a Family Feel 🌊🏄‍♀️

From the moment I arrived, I felt completely welcomed — like part of a big surf family. The vibe at the camp is warm, genuine, and full of good energy. The team is incredibly experienced and knowledgeable, always making sure you feel safe and supported in the water, no matter your skill level.

Shout out to Nadiw, Mara, Chuety and Tonic for making everyday an adventure, teaching me so much and making me feel safe while challenging myself! Incredible people and incredible teachers.

The food was an absolute highlight — gourmet-quality meals made with so much care and creativity. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented.

The accommodation was also extremely comfortable, with every detail thoughtfully taken care of to make you feel at home. Whether you’re here to learn, improve your surfing, or just relax and soak up the atmosphere, this place truly has it all.

Highly recommend — I left feeling both recharged and inspired!

Name: Weligama Bay
User_Review: had an amazing time at The Surfers! The camp has such a relaxed and friendly vibe, you feel welcome from the very first day. The volunteers do an awesome job and really make sure everyone feels comfortable and included. The surf instructors are top-notch, super skilled, patient, and passionate about what they do. I learned a lot in a short time and had so much fun in every session. Definitely recommend this place to anyone who wants to learn surfing or just enjoy good vibes by the ocean!

Name: Weligama Bay
User_Review: That’s not my first time in Surfer and for sure not the last one! I like community gatherings, surfing lessons, coaches, food, accommodation, everything is well organised. The only thing is it is better to visit Sri Lanka not in October but later:

Name: Matara Paravi Duwa Temple
User_Review:
Peaceful and Scenic – Parewi Duwa Temple, Matara

Parewi Duwa Temple in Matara is a beautiful and serene Buddhist temple uniquely located on a small island connected to the mainland by a footbridge. The temple offers a peaceful atmosphere perfect for meditation and reflection, with stunning views of the Indian Ocean all around. Surrounded by waves and sea breeze, it’s a must-visit spiritual and cultural spot for those exploring southern Sri Lanka. The surroundings are clean and well-maintained, and the temple has historical and religious significance for Buddhists. A truly picturesque and calming place.

Name: Matara Paravi Duwa Temple
User_Review:
Parewi Duwa Viharaya covers the entirety of the Parewi Dupatha (meaning Pigeon Island), a tiny islet off the coast of Matara in the south of Sri Lanka. This islet is connected to the mainland by a pedestrian bridge.
The stupa atop the temple is very striking with a golden casket and colourfully painted walls and roof.
The view from the top is exhilarating.
The temple sadly needs better maintenance and housekeeping.

Name: Matara Paravi Duwa Temple
User_Review:
The Matara Paravi Duwa Temple, also known as Uposathaghara Raja Maha Viharaya, is a serene Buddhist temple situated on Pigeon Island, just off the coast of Matara in southern Sri Lanka. Connected to the mainland by a pedestrian bridge, the temple offers visitors a tranquil escape amidst the bustling city.

This temple, though not ancient, holds significant cultural and spiritual value. It was established to serve as a place of Buddhist worship and has become a symbol of resilience, especially after undergoing restorations following the 2004 tsunami. The temple's location on an island provides a peaceful environment ideal for meditation and reflection.

Visitors are greeted by a golden Buddha statue and can explore the temple's sacred halls, which are adorned with various Buddhist artifacts. The temple's unique setting, surrounded by the Indian Ocean, offers panoramic views and a sense of spiritual fulfillment to all who visit.

Access to the temple is free, and it's open to all visitors. However, it's important to dress modestly and remove footwear before entering the temple premises, respecting the local customs and religious practices.
Sri Lanka Travel Pages

Whether you're a pilgrim seeking peace or a traveler looking for a unique cultural experience, the Matara Paravi Duwa Temple offers a harmonious blend of spirituality, history, and natural beauty.

Name: Matara Paravi Duwa Temple
User_Review: Ugly, badly maintained , looks more like an abandoned site than a real temple. There is a compulsory donation fee to enter, and a surf water pond with green water full of mosquitoes  to make it feel less appealing. The view from a distant is more than enough

Name: Matara Paravi Duwa Temple
User_Review: Peaceful and beautiful place in the middle of the sea. Easy to reach by bridge. Perfect spot to relax and worship. Must-visit in Matara.

Name: Tangalle Beach
User_Review:
It's okay.
Strong wind cause strong waves.
Bit of wild beach suits for surfing not for child playing.

Name: Tangalle Beach
User_Review: It is a very beautiful beach. If you are looking for a quiet beach, this is not the place for you. The reason is that many people come to this beach to watch and swim. Most of them are local tourists. But you can have fun with your family. The water is quite dirty. If you come here to swim, you also have normal facilities to washout sea water. In my opinion, if you have not been here before, you can definitely choose this as a destination.

Name: Tangalle Beach
User_Review: South part of this beach is sheltered by rocks, so that it's good to swim even in summer when waves are big

Name: Tangalle Beach
User_Review:
The beach is quiet and decent. Areas around the rocks are but dirty and most likely local kids have dumped some trash like soda cans. The beach is usually accessible by anyone, however if you stay at one of the hotels on the beach or dine at restaurants or cafes, they grant you access to sunbeds for free.
It’s not a crowded beach and quite a lot of fun to play even for small kids but ofcourse under adult‘s watch. I would also consider swimming quite safe in the beach even though there are occasional huge waves

Name: Tangalle Beach
User_Review: Local part of the tangalle beach. Not too touristy. It's quite a small beach, but plenty of room. The sea is not wild and suitable for swimming. There are some trees, so you can hide in the shade which is very nice. The constant sea breeze is great. Very refreshing. Nearby, there's a juice bar and they serve juices with ice cream. Amazing

Name: Galle Lighthouse
User_Review:
We visited the Galle Fort Lighthouse as a couple with our 10-year-old, and it turned out to be one of the highlights of our trip! Watching the sunset by the lighthouse was absolutely magical — the sky turning shades of gold and pink over the ocean made it a picture-perfect moment.

The area around the lighthouse is peaceful yet lively, with people strolling along the ramparts, enjoying the breeze, and capturing photos. Our kid loved exploring the spot and watching the waves crash against the rocks below.

It’s a must-visit when in Galle Fort — especially during sunset. The view, the atmosphere, and the history all come together to create an unforgettable experience. 🌅⚓️🇱🇰

Name: Galle Lighthouse
User_Review:
The oldest lighthouse in Sri Lanka, built originally by the British, it's located within Galle fort with breathtaking views of the sunset and ocean in general. It is walkable from the main entrance to the fort, and the fort area in general is beautiful all around so it's worth the walk.
Go here in the evenings or mornings for nice sea breeze and dawn / dusk views. The heat during other parts of the day can be dissuading, so go prepared with hydration and sun protection.

Name: Galle Lighthouse
User_Review:
Galle Lighthouse
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ | Sunset Views & Ocean Charm
The Galle Lighthouse, standing tall on the southeastern tip of the fort, offers spectacular Indian Ocean vistas and is perfect for photography enthusiasts.
Highlights: Sunset walks along the ramparts, the lighthouse itself, and panoramic views of the bay.

Nearby Spots: Flag Rock Bastion and small cafés—ideal for a relaxed snack or coffee.
Tip: Best visited late afternoon to sunset for soft lighting and fewer crowds.
Even if you’re short on time, the lighthouse alone is worth the stroll—it captures the essence of the fort’s coastal charm.

Name: Galle Lighthouse
User_Review: The most iconic light house inside the fortress. The place looks stunning at the morning and the evening(Golden hour). This whole place looks medieval type of buildings even the coffee shops. Portuguese era buildings and stunning breeze are the perfect things to relax. Free parking available all the time. That is the best thing. At the night this become even more beautiful.

Name: Galle Lighthouse
User_Review:
Dutch built structure. Visitors cannot enter inside of the building.
Light beam can be clearly seen during night time. Not very crowded. Very photogenic place.

Name: Galle Face Green
User_Review:
Galle Face Green in Colombo is one of those rare places where the vibrant energy of a city meets the calm of the ocean. Stretching for nearly half a kilometer along the Indian Ocean, this open green promenade is the largest seafront space in the city. It’s a favorite spot for both locals and tourists to relax, enjoy the sea breeze, fly kites, or simply sit by the water and watch life go by.

What makes Galle Face Green special is its atmosphere. On one side, you have the bustling Colombo skyline, and on the other, endless waves crashing against the sea wall. In between, families picnic on the grass, children run freely, and vendors sell a variety of street food. From spicy isso vade (prawn fritters) to fresh mango slices sprinkled with chili and salt, the food here adds a lively and authentic touch to the experience. The evenings are especially beautiful when the wind picks up and the sunset paints the sky with golden and crimson hues.

Though it’s not a sandy beach where you can swim, Galle Face Green offers something unique—an open, welcoming space where the community gathers. It’s best visited in the late afternoon or evening, when the heat softens and the place truly comes alive.
If you are in Colombo, Galle Face Green is a must-visit. It’s not just about the ocean views, but also about soaking in the spirit of the city, enjoying simple pleasures, and sharing the space with people from all walks of life.

Name: Galle Face Green
User_Review: Looks lovely at night because of the lights of the high rises facing it. Late evening or at night are the bests times to visit because, it is hot during the day time and there is no place to take shelter from the scorching sun. There is no sandy beach below to dip your feet to cool off either.  The cool breeze blowing across the Indian Ocean is refreshing at night time however.

Name: Galle Face Green
User_Review:
🌅 Galle Face Green – Colombo’s Iconic Seaside Promenade
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

A vibrant and timeless public space where locals and tourists gather to enjoy the ocean breeze, street food, and golden sunsets — Galle Face Green is the cultural heart of Colombo’s evenings and weekends.

🚶 Arrival & Atmosphere
Arriving in the late afternoon, you instantly feel the energy. Families, couples, joggers, and street vendors bring life to the seafront. Many bring mats to sit on the grass, relax, and enjoy the fresh breeze. The open space and sea air offer a refreshing break from the city.
Originally laid out in the 19th century during colonial times, this stretch has transformed into Colombo’s most iconic gathering ground.

🪁 Family Fun & Activities
Kite Flying – Vendors sell kites starting around LKR 300, with larger and artistic designs available at higher prices. Watching the colorful sky filled with kites is magical.
Kids’ Entertainment – Remote-control car rides, play zones, and ice cream stalls keep children happily engaged.

Evening Events – On weekends, the promenade often hosts cultural shows, street musicians, and seasonal food festivals.

🍢 Street Food & Local Flavors
From crispy isso wade (prawn fritters) and spicy kottu to fresh fruits and ice cream, the food stalls are a true highlight. Affordable, authentic, and full of flavor — perfect for a casual bite while enjoying the sea view.

📸 Views & Photography
The sunset over the Indian Ocean paints the sky in gold, orange, and pink. With ships on the horizon and flags fluttering along the promenade, it’s one of Colombo’s most photogenic spots.

Locals often come dressed for photoshoots, adding even more color and character to the scene.

🏙️ Nearby Highlights
Galle Face Green is surrounded by some of Colombo’s top hotels, malls, and landmarks:

One Galle Face Mall – Luxury shopping, dining, and cinema

Port City Colombo – Modern urban development with walkways and cycling paths

ITC Ratnadeepa – A rising luxury hotel complex

Galle Face Hotel – Historic colonial-era icon, ideal for high tea or sunset cocktails

Shangri-La Colombo – Modern luxury with excellent dining and views

The Kingsbury Hotel – Known for buffets, rooftop bars, and elegant dining

This makes Galle Face Green not only a cultural landmark but also a gateway to Colombo’s luxury, shopping, and lifestyle hub.

🅿️ Practical Tips
Best Time to Visit: Late afternoon until sunset for cooler weather and vibrant atmosphere.
Parking: Limited, but available near the Galle Face Hotel and One Galle Face Mall.
Accessibility: Open, flat spaces with wheelchair and stroller access. Security patrols ensure it’s safe for families.

♻️ Respect Nature & Cleanliness
⚠️ Please avoid littering and dispose of garbage responsibly. Protect our environment — never throw rubbish into the sea. Keeping Galle Face Green clean ensures everyone can enjoy its beauty for generations to come.

✅ Final Thoughts
More than a park, Galle Face Green is a cultural and historical landmark that blends everyday Sri Lankan life with coastal charm. Whether you’re here for a walk, a snack, a kite, or simply to relax by the waves, it offers a memorable and authentic experience.

🌟 Highly recommended for anyone seeking a lively, scenic, and flavorful evening by the sea.

Name: Galle Face Green
User_Review: Great energy even at late night on Sundays. Place was full of people and everyone minding their own business. Lot of small shops for snacks and beverages. What made it even great were local kite flyers who brought long tail Chinese kites filling the night sky with vibrant colours. Feels pretty safe and very cleans. Kudos to people of Colombo for keeping the public beach so clean and safe for tourists

Name: Galle Face Green
User_Review: Stunning place for a sea side stroll to catch amazing  sunset views. Easy to get to by tuk tuk or uber and across the road from One Galle Face Mall for shopping or dining afterward

Name: Colombo Lotus Tower
User_Review:
Visiting Lotus Tower was truly a memorable experience! The architecture is stunning, and the view from the top is absolutely breathtaking you can see the entire city and coastline in one panoramic glance.

The facilities are modern and well maintained, and the staff were friendly, helpful, and professional throughout our visit. The atmosphere is both exciting and relaxing, making it perfect for sightseeing or spending quality time with family and friends.

A must visit attraction in Colombo for anyone who wants to experience the city from a unique perspective. Highly recommended!

Name: Colombo Lotus Tower
User_Review:
Visiting the Colombo Lotus Tower is an experience that mixes modern ambition with architectural symbolism. Rising prominently above Colombo’s skyline, the tower is designed to resemble a lotus flower—a culturally meaningful symbol of purity and growth. Its sleek green stem and petal-like crown add elegance to the cityscape, especially when lit up at night.
The tower offers more than just a striking exterior. Inside, there are multiple levels housing a telecommunications museum, exhibition halls, and leisure spaces. One of the most exciting attractions is the observation deck, which gives panoramic views of Colombo and Beira Lake from a height. Another highlight is the revolving restaurant—dining there while slowly rotating offers a continually changing vista of the city, making every meal memorable.

The structure also serves its practical purpose as a broadcasting and communications hub, which helps integrate utility with tourism. Some of the interiors and facilities are state-of-the-art, though in parts you can notice that it’s still a relatively new project, and there may be growing pains in staff management, crowd handling, or maintenance.

Overall, the Lotus Tower stands as a landmark of ambition for Sri Lanka. It’s worth visiting for the views, the architectural design, and as a symbol of modern Colombo. If you have limited time, make sure to go during late afternoon or early evening so you can catch daylight views and also see it illuminated after dusk.

Name: Colombo Lotus Tower
User_Review: I visited the Lotus Tower during the daytime, and it was a really nice experience. The staff were friendly and guided us from the entrance to the exit. We went up by lift, which was smooth and quick. From the top, you get a wide view of Colombo city — you can clearly see the Port City, prominent hotels, schools, churches, kovils, mosques, the railway station and yard, and many other landmarks. It’s also a great place for capturing photos. The area is clean, well organized, and totally worth the LKR 500 ticket for locals(2025 october)

Name: Colombo Lotus Tower
User_Review:
Great place.
30 stories up, and gives you full view of colombo. Some parts of Kandy, Sigiria and much more.

View is breathtaking, def recommend a visit if you have time in Colombo..

Ticket might be slightly higher for tourists.
It costed 6100 rupees per person.

Name: Colombo Lotus Tower
User_Review:
I recently had dinner at Blue Orbit, Lotus Tower, and it was an absolutely spectacular experience. The food was excellent with a wide variety of options to choose from, and every dish was beautifully presented. The flavors and quality exceeded expectations, making it well worth the price paid.

The environment was elegant and welcoming, and the night view was truly a feast for the eyes, making the evening even more memorable with my family. Even though it was a buffet, the staff were exceptionally attentive and looked after our needs with genuine care, which added so much to the overall experience.

Overall, I had a wonderful time and would highly recommend Blue Orbit at Lotus Tower for anyone looking for great food, a breathtaking view, and outstanding service.

Name: Gangaramaya Temple
User_Review:
Rising gracefully beside the tranquil waters of Beira Lake, the Gangaramaya Temple stands as one of Colombo’s most treasured landmarks — a living blend of spirituality, history, and culture. Its story begins in the late 19th century, when the revered monk Venerable Hikkaduwe Sri Sumangala Nayaka Thera founded it as part of a great Buddhist revival movement sweeping through Sri Lanka. What started as a modest shrine grew steadily into a vibrant temple complex, serving not only as a place of worship but also as a center of education, charity, and community life.

Stepping into Gangaramaya is like walking through a kaleidoscope of cultures. The temple’s architecture is an eclectic fusion of Sri Lankan, Thai, Indian, and Chinese influences, reflected in its golden pagodas, intricate carvings, and vivid murals. The Seema Malaka, an elegant assembly hall floating serenely on Beira Lake, was designed by Sri Lanka’s legendary architect Geoffrey Bawa, offering a peaceful refuge for meditation. Inside the main temple, one encounters an astonishing museum of treasures — rows of Buddha statues from across Asia, antique coins, elaborate gifts from devotees around the world, and even vintage cars that tell stories of eras long past.

Beyond its architectural beauty, Gangaramaya Temple pulses with life and purpose. It has become a cornerstone of Colombo’s Buddhist community, supporting orphans, vocational trainees, and the elderly through its social service initiatives. Each year, in February, the temple bursts into color and rhythm during the Gangaramaya Navam Perahera, a magnificent procession of elephants, dancers, and drummers that winds through the city, celebrating the enduring spirit of Buddhism. And during Vesak, the temple glows under thousands of lanterns, transforming into a sea of light and devotion.

For visitors, Gangaramaya offers a rare blend of reverence and wonder. Modest dress is expected — shoulders and knees covered, shoes removed — as a sign of respect. Whether you come to meditate, marvel at its artistry, or simply watch the golden reflections on Beira Lake, this temple promises a glimpse into Sri Lanka’s spiritual soul. In the heart of a bustling modern city, Gangaramaya remains a sanctuary of peace — where the ancient and the contemporary, the sacred and the worldly, coexist in perfect harmony.

Name: Gangaramaya Temple
User_Review: A beautiful and peaceful temple in the middle of Colombo. The golden Buddha statues, calm lake surroundings, and city lights create a stunning and serene atmosphere. It’s well maintained, quiet, and perfect for evening visits or moments of reflection. A must-visit spot to experience both spirituality and the beauty of the city.

Name: Gangaramaya Temple
User_Review:
Here’s the story of Gangaramaya Temple (Gangaramaya Vihara) in Colombo 🌸:

⸻

📜 Origins & Early History
•	The temple was founded in the late 19th century by the famous scholar-monk Hikkaduwe Sri Sumangala Nayaka Thera.
•	At that time, Colombo was developing rapidly under colonial rule, and the temple became a center for Buddhist education and culture.

⸻

🌸 Growth & Significance
•	The temple’s development was continued under Venerable Galaboda Gnanissara Thero (“Podi Hamuduruwo”), who expanded it into what it is today.
•	Unlike many traditional temples, Gangaramaya became a mix of religious, educational, and cultural institution.
•	It served as a learning center for monks and laypeople, teaching Buddhism, vocational training, and even social services.

⸻

🏛️ Architecture & Collections
•	The temple is famous for its fusion of Sri Lankan, Thai, Indian, and Chinese styles.
•	Inside, you’ll find:
•	Hundreds of Buddha statues (from Sri Lanka and abroad).
•	A Bodhi tree grown from a sapling of the sacred Anuradhapura tree.
•	An eclectic museum-like collection of antiques, gifts, ivory carvings, coins, even vintage cars.
•	A library and educational halls.

⸻

🙏 Cultural & Social Role
•	Gangaramaya is not just a temple — it has played a big role in Colombo’s Buddhist revival during colonial times.
•	It runs orphanages, vocational training programs, and charitable projects.
•	Every year, it hosts the Navam Perahera, one of Colombo’s grandest Buddhist processions, with elephants, dancers, and drummers.

⸻

✨ Today
•	The temple remains one of Colombo’s most visited sites — both as a place of worship and as a tourist attraction.
•	It is sometimes called a “living museum” because of its unusual blend of the sacred and the worldly.

⸻

Would you like me to create a short storytelling version of Gangaramaya’s history — something you could use for a tour guide script or social media caption?

Name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
User_Review:
The Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara is far more than just a temple—it is a spectacularly well-maintained living monument with a breathtaking legacy that stretches back over 2,500 years! This site is essential for anyone interested in the history of Sri Lanka and Buddhism.

The temple's antiquity is palpable, but its presentation is incredibly clear. All the historical and religious details are beautifully conveyed, particularly through the stunning artwork. The detailed and vibrant drawings on the walls are nothing short of a masterpiece, clearly telling the profound story of Lord Buddha's visit to Sri Lanka around 500 B.C.

Walking through the shrine and viewing these murals is like opening an ancient, illuminated chronicle. It’s a truly awe-inspiring experience that provides deep insight into the island's spiritual heart. A must-visit pilgrimage site and a major cultural treasure!

Name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
User_Review: Kelaniya Raja Maha Viharaya, located near Colombo, is one of Sri Lanka’s most sacred Buddhist temples. Believed to have been visited by Lord Buddha, it is famed for its stunning murals by Soliyas Mendis and peaceful atmosphere. The temple’s annual Duruthu Perahera attracts many devotees, making it a cherished site of faith, culture, and beauty.

Name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
User_Review:
A sacred and historic Buddhist temple just outside Colombo, Kelaniya Raja Maha Viharaya is known for its spiritual atmosphere, beautiful stupa, and detailed murals. The surroundings are clean and well-maintained, with a calm riverside setting that adds to the peace.

It’s usually open from 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM, and mornings or evenings are best to visit to avoid crowds and heat. Plenty of parking is available, and the temple grounds are spacious and serene. A great place to reflect or experience a piece of Sri Lanka’s cultural soul.

Name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
User_Review:
The Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara or Kelaniya Temple is a Buddhist temple in Kelaniya, Sri Lanka. It is located 11 km (6.8 mi) north-east of Colombo. The current chief incumbent (chief priest) is Venerable Professor Kollupitiye Mahinda Sangharakkhitha Thera.

The temple has often been associated with the rise and fall of Sri Lanka, with the popular saying that as the Kelaniya temple rose, Sri Lanka rose and as it fell, the country and its administration fell. It has thus had a deep association with the political powers of the country. As such, the temple is frequently visited by politicians both government and opposition.

Buddhists believe the temple to have been hallowed during the third and final visit of the Buddha to Sri Lanka, eight years after gaining enlightenment.[3] Its history would thus go back to before 500 BCE. The Mahawansa records that the original Stupa at Kelaniya enshrined a gem-studded throne on which the Buddha sat and preached. The temple flourished during the Kotte era but much of its land was confiscated during the Portuguese empire. Under the Dutch empire, however, there were new gifts of land and under the patronage of King Kirthi Sri Rajasingha the temple was rebuilt. It was refurbished in the first half of the 20th century with the help of Helena Wijewardene.
The Kelaniya Stupa enshrined a gem-studded throne on which the Buddha sat and preached. Buddha's second visit to Nagadeepaya, Sri Lanka was in the fifth year of enlightenment to settle a dispute between two Naga Kings, Chulodara and Mahodara over the possession of a Gem Studded throne. After having listened to the Dhamma sermons by Buddha the Naga kings paid homage to Buddha with overwhelming faith and the throne was offered to Buddha by two Naga kings.

Name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
User_Review:
Kelaniya Raja Maha Viharaya is one of the most important Buddhist temples in Sri Lanka, located about 10 km northeast of Colombo, on the banks of the Kelani River.

A few key points about it:

Historical significance – According to tradition, the Lord Buddha visited this site on his third visit to Sri Lanka, about 2,500 years ago.

Sacred relic – It’s believed to enshrine a gem-studded throne on which the Lord Buddha sat during his visit.

Architecture & art – The temple is famous for its beautiful murals by artist Solias Mendis, depicting scenes from the Buddha’s life and Sri Lankan history.

Annual Perahera – The Kelaniya Duruthu Perahera in January is one of the island’s grandest Buddhist processions, with dancers, drummers, and decorated elephants.

It’s not just a religious site but also a symbol of Sri Lanka’s cultural heritage.

If you are interested in ancient paintings, visit the Kelaniya Raja Maha Viharaya at least once in your lifetime.

Name: Kalutara Bodhiya
User_Review: An iconic, majestic white stupa (pagoda) located on the banks of the Kalu River, right at the northern entrance to Kalutara city. It is famous for being one of the world's few hollow Buddhist stupas, allowing visitors to walk inside its vast dome. The site is a highly venerated place of worship, located directly opposite the sacred Kalutara Bodhiya (Bo Tree). The Bo tree is believed to be a direct sapling of the Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi in Anuradhapura, which itself originated from the tree under which Lord Buddha attained enlightenment. The stupa and Bo tree create a powerful spiritual landmark at the entrance to Kalutara. Many travelers stop here to seek blessings for a safe journey. A must-see landmark in the region.

Name: Kalutara Bodhiya
User_Review:
13.09.2025
By the side of the Galle main road this temple with its iconic pagoda is a place that cannot be missed.
The temple has the stupa inside of which the devotees can visit and offer flowers.

There is a Buddhist bookshop inside the premises and it had beautiful postcards of Sri Lanka for a reasonable price (50 Rs each).

You are not allowed to take pictures inside the stupa and they have mentioned it too. From inside the stupa, the beautiful Kalu river and the beach can be seen.
Below the stupa is a museum which gives an idea about the history of the Kalutara temple and how it was preserved for generations.

There is a tunnel below to cross the road to reach the Bho tree which is at the other side of the main road.

Kalutara Boh tree is known to be 2300 years old (as per the information shown in the museum).

The car park to the temple is far away and you need to walk a long way from there to the temple.
