Name: Thiruketheeswaram Temple
User_Review:
This kovil is also called " Thiru Ketheswaram Kovil."" It is located in Mannar district, Sri Lanka. It is the oldest kovil in Sri Lanka. It has over 2400 years of evidence.

Thirukithesewaram is the one of "pancha Ishwaram kovil built in dedication to Hindu supreme being "Ishwara" form of "Shiva" Pancha Ishwaram Kovil are five hindu temples that are located in coastal areas.
1.) Thirukithesewaram /Ketheswaram kovil - Mannar
2.) Naguleswara kovil - Keeramalai
3.) Koneswaram kovil - Trincomalee
4.) Munneswaram kovil - Chilaw
5.) Tondeswaram kovil - Dondra

Kings Pallawa, Pandya, and Chola developed this kovil. But in 1575, Portuguese was completely destroyed this kovil. So in 1583, "pujas" of this kovil was terminated.

But in 1903, this kovil was rebuilt at its original site.

Thirukoneswaram Kovil in Trincomalee is one of the most beautiful and spiritually significant places to visit in Sri Lanka. The temple is located on a breathtaking cliff surrounded by the Indian Ocean, offering spectacular views of the sea crashing against the rocks below. The atmosphere here is serene, making it not only a place of worship but also a destination to experience peace, history, and natural beauty all in one.

At the entrance, a large and majestic Shiva statue stands proudly, welcoming visitors and adding to the sacred atmosphere. This impressive statue is a highlight and a perfect place to pause for photos and reflection before heading into the temple.

The surroundings are full of life and charm. You will often see spotted deer wandering freely in the area, and visitors can even feed them, which is an unforgettable experience. Monkeys are also a common sight, adding to the liveliness of the environment, although you should be cautious with food and belongings around them.

From the parking area, it takes about a 10-minute walk to reach the temple. This short walk is enjoyable as it passes through a lively pathway filled with small shops selling souvenirs, ornaments, religious items, fresh fruits, and cool drinks. The walk itself feels like part of the experience, offering a glimpse into local culture and traditions.

Overall, Thirukoneswaram Kovil is a must-visit attraction in Trincomalee, combining spiritual significance, natural beauty, wildlife encounters, and a touch of local culture in one unforgettable visit.

Name: Koneswaram Kovil
User_Review: Standing tall on Swami Rock in Trincomalee, Thirukoneswaram is more than a temple—it's a timeless symbol of devotion and legend. Believed to be over 2,000 years old, the temple is one of the Pancha Ishwarams—five coastal Shiva temples said to be built by divine sages and kings. This sacred site overlooks the vast Indian Ocean, blending myth, history, and breathtaking beauty. Once destroyed by the Portuguese, today it rises again in peace, prayer, and faith.

Name: Koneswaram Kovil
User_Review: Perched atop Swami Rock, Koneswaram Kovil offers breathtaking views of the Indian Ocean. The temple is open daily from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM, welcoming all visitors with a modest dress code and a serene atmosphere.Free parking is available near the temple entrance, though it can get busy during peak times.

Name: Koneswaram Kovil
User_Review:
Visiting the Shankari Devi Shakti Peetham in Trincomalee, Sri Lanka, was a profoundly spiritual experience. Nestled beside the majestic Koneswaram Temple on a cliff overlooking the Indian Ocean, this sacred site, one of the 18 Maha Shakti Peethas, radiates divine energy. Though the original temple was destroyed by Portuguese invaders in the 17th century, the rebuilt shrine still holds immense significance, with a serene ambiance enhanced by the sound of waves and the sight of the Bilva tree against the ocean backdrop.
The temple’s rituals, including Puja and Arathi, are performed with great devotion, creating a peaceful and blissful atmosphere. The presence of monkeys and deer around the temple adds a charming touch of nature. The location is less crowded on weekdays, making it ideal for quiet reflection. I recommend visiting between September and February for pleasant weather. The dress code is traditional—sarees or churidar for women, dhoti or trousers for men—so plan accordingly. A must-visit for spiritual seekers and those drawn to the divine legacy of Shankari Devi

Name: Koneswaram Kovil
User_Review:
Koneswaram Temple or Tirukoneswaram Kovil is situated on top of Swami Rock. This temple is dedicated to the Hindu God Shiva, and it is one out of five such temples situated around Sri Lanka. It is believed that this Kovil has been in existence since 2500 years ago, and was renovated by the South Indian Chola King Kulakottan and maintained by other Sinhala Buddhist Kings. In 1622, the Portuguese destroyed this temple which they referred to as the temple of a thousand columns by shoving the temple over the edge of the cliff into the deep blue seas below. The revered Shiva lingam (mark of Shiva) was subsequently rescued by divers. The Portuguese used the building materials of the temple in the construction of Fort Fredrick.

Car parking place is near very closer to Kovil hence people who has difficulties in walking even can visit this beautiful place. Many wild animals like monkeys, deers and variety of birds can be seen living peacefully

Name: Marble Beach Trincomalee
User_Review:
When you first arrive at Mable Beach, the entry process is pretty simple. You purchase a ticket for just Rs. 50, which gives you access to both the hotel grounds and the public beach. After that, you drive forward and reach a Y-junction take the left turn for the hotel or the right turn for the public beach.

The public beach is free with your ticket. It’s nice but can get quite busy during long weekends or public holidays. On a regular day though, it’s fairly quiet with just a few people around.

We decided to check out the hotel side, which is partially managed by the Sri Lanka Air Force. They offer a really good day-out package for Rs. 2000, which is worth it in my opinion. With this, you get:

* Access to their private beach (only for hotel guests),
* A buffet meal,
* Use of changing rooms,
* Plenty of comfortable seating by the beach,
* Plus, optional activities like scuba diving and water sports in the mornings.

The private beach is spotless, peaceful, and not crowded at all a huge contrast to the public side. The views are stunning, and it’s the kind of place you can really relax without interruptions. The staff were extremely friendly and accommodating, which made the whole experience even better.

The hotel also has rooms you can book on a full-board basis. While we didn’t stay overnight, the rooms looked clean and decent, making it a nice option for a longer getaway.

Overall i would say Mable Beach turned out to be a really refreshing escape in Trincomalee. Whether you just want a quick dip in the public beach or a more relaxed, all-inclusive day by the private shore, there’s something here for everyone. For us, the day package was definitely worth it clean beach, good food, and great vibes.

Name: Marble Beach Trincomalee
User_Review: A truly beautiful and relaxing beach with stunning blue water. Giving it 4 stars due to the overly large and sometimes disruptive crowds. Highly recommend for the scenery, but expect it to be very busy.

Name: Marble Beach Trincomalee
User_Review:
A stunning, quiet beach with minimal facilities. Entrance fee is 50 rupees only. Feels very safe and its beautiful. Beach looks like it's kept clean.
There are toilets, not cleanest but not stinky, so still okay (ALWAYS BRING TOILET PAPER!)
Theres a drinks and tiny snacks shop (crisps), but no real food. Better to bring some food with you if you hate walking in the heat.
I was one of the few ladies in bikini there, but I felt okay. I saw some navy/police guy patrolling there, I think that's why it stays calm.

In a 10-15 mins walk you can get to Marble Beach Resort to eat good, diverse food. I read a lot of negative reviews about that restaurant but the food I ordered there was good and prepared quickly.

Name: Marble Beach Trincomalee
User_Review: Marble Beach is beautiful but can not see any waves. It is like a tank but anyone can swim and enjoy the sea water there. In order to enter you have to buy a ticket which is really affordable ( 0.1 - 0.3US$ ). Vehicle parking is also free. You can find drinking water for free too.

Name: Marble Beach Trincomalee
User_Review: Beautiful quiet sandy beach suitable for swimming 40 minute drive from Trincomalee. It costs 20 rupees per person (August 2025) to enter via a Police gate. There’s a small snorkelling centre on the beach, but the places to buy food and drink were closed when we visited. Also don’t be put off by the rubbish strewn car park. The beach is clean and largely litter free.

Name: Batticaloa Lagoon
User_Review:
Very bad place to visit.
It's an government area. But, some people took the power to control it.
They are charging for parking bikes on the road.

Name: Batticaloa Lagoon
User_Review: The place might have been nice in the past but it's not the case any more. If you want to rent a kayak, try doing it through some hotels. We went there to rent a kayak but the owner told us there was too much wind. We were only allowed to rent a paddle boat which could have use maintenance. We tried for 15 minutes but the gear were making so much noise that we have up. We actually spoke with two people on a kayak from a hotel who were doing fine...

Name: Batticaloa Lagoon
User_Review:
Beautiful location, best for an evening kayaking session when the sun goes down. Some times of the year jelly fish come to these waters and might be dangerous for swimmers.
Boats, kayaking rides available.

Name: Batticaloa Lagoon
User_Review: It's a beautiful lagoon with great view and has small island. Boat tours are offered  around the island and to the sea and to see the city .no swimming though .BBQ and other entertainments available on request. Great place to visit with family and kids .

Name: Batticaloa Lagoon
User_Review: Probably evening time is better to spend your leisure time here. there are stone benches available to sit.

Name: Dutch Fort Batticaloa
User_Review:
A peaceful historical gem by the lagoon!

The Batticaloa Dutch Fort is a beautiful slice of history tucked between the lagoon and the town. The views from the fort walls are serene — perfect for sunset photography or a quiet stroll. You can really feel the colonial history here, especially with the remaining structures and old stonework. It’s not very crowded, which makes it a great place to unwind and explore.

Would be even better with a few info boards or guided tours, but still a must-visit if you’re in Batticaloa!

Name: Dutch Fort Batticaloa
User_Review:
The fort is an interesting place that reflects the rich history of Batticaloa. The views from the top of the fort are beautiful, offering views of the town as well as the lagoon. Our tour guide was very knowledgeable and showed us all parts of the fort. We were also able to purchase handmade baskets from one of the stores inside the fort on the ground floor.

The government should invest in maintaining these places of historical value in the Eastern coast, as they do in other parts of Sri Lanka.

As tourists, it is a worthwhile visit not just to learn about the history but also to support the preservation of such places.

Name: Dutch Fort Batticaloa
User_Review:
We visited the Dutch Fort in Batticaloa, which is currently under the care of the Department of Archaeology, Sri Lanka. The site today functions as the Divisional Secretariat, so while it retains its historical significance, it is also an active administrative location.

The outer walls of the fort remain intact, showcasing the original colonial era construction. However, most of the internal buildings have been reconstructed, so you will not find the full original structures.

It is still a good place to visit if you are travelling in Batticaloa, especially for those interested in history and colonial architecture. The fort gives a glimpse into the region’s past, and its location makes it a convenient stop for a short visit.

Name: Dutch Fort Batticaloa
User_Review: Batticaloa Fort is a fascinating historical site that beautifully showcases Sri Lanka’s colonial past. Surrounded by water on three sides, the fort offers scenic views along with a quiet, peaceful atmosphere. Walking through the old ramparts and structures gives you a glimpse into centuries of history, while the surrounding lagoon adds to the charm. It’s less crowded than many other forts in the country, making it a calm and enjoyable place to explore. A must-visit if you’re in Batticaloa and want to experience both history and natural beauty.

Name: Dutch Fort Batticaloa
User_Review:
Located in a place where you can see  Kalladi bridge clearly.
Not much people and parking was not an issue.
Appreciate the initiative to remove the offices from the premises and thinking of preserving it.
Good place to visit.

Name: Pasikuda Beach
User_Review:
Pasikuda Beach is absolutely pristine. Most of the coastline is quiet and peaceful, except for the public entrance area which can get a bit crowded. The sand is soft and white, with a few rocky spots here and there.

The views are stunning, both at sunrise and sunset – truly breathtaking, almost like being on a different planet. One of the most beautiful beaches in Sri Lanka.

Name: Pasikuda Beach
User_Review: This is one of the most popular beaches in the Eastern Province. It's a beautiful beach with calm, warm & clear water and a long stretch of soft white sandy shore. This is a perfect place for a relaxing evening bath and a stroll. I visited it with friends a few years ago and thoroughly enjoyed the outing.

Name: Pasikuda Beach
User_Review:
Amoung the luxury accomodation with their private beaches, there is a lovely space for everyone at the public beach. That has access to baths, changing facilities, watersports, etc.

Just away from here, the coral reef is dying and there is tons of broken away corals.

Visit Pasikuda if you get the chance.

Name: Pasikuda Beach
User_Review:
Pasikuda Beach (sometimes called Pasimudah Beach) is one of those spots that just feels like a tropical escape from the moment you arrive. I spent a few days here and honestly, it was such a chill and beautiful experience — definitely worth the visit if you’re exploring Sri Lanka’s east coast.

First off, the beach itself is absolutely stunning. The sand is powdery white and incredibly soft underfoot – perfect for barefoot strolls or just lounging under a palm tree. The water is shallow for quite a stretch, so it’s super safe and ideal for a relaxed swim or just floating around and soaking up the sun.

One of the things I loved most was how calm it was — no strong waves or crazy winds. The whole atmosphere is peaceful and laid-back, perfect if you're looking to just unwind. The lack of wind also makes it more enjoyable to sit by the water without getting blasted by sand or chilly breezes.

Snorkeling here is a nice bonus! The water is super clear, and even though it's not the most vibrant coral reef in Sri Lanka, you can still spot a few colourful fish and underwater life around the shallow areas. Definitely bring a snorkel set if you have one!

There are also some great beach resorts along the coast — ranging from mid-range to more luxurious options — and most of them open right up to the beach. It’s the kind of place where you wake up, walk a few steps, and you're on the sand. Many resorts also offer little extras like cocktails on the beach, seafood dinners, and spa treatments, which just adds to the whole relaxing vibe.

On top of that, the local people are genuinely friendly and welcoming. Whether it's resort staff, shop owners, or people just walking by, everyone had a smile and was happy to help with directions or recommend a good place to eat.

Pasikuda is a great beach if you want white sand, warm shallow waters, peace and quiet, and some light snorkeling. It’s not overcrowded or commercialised, which makes it feel even more special. If you're in Sri Lanka during the east coast season (April–September), definitely make time for a stop here. Perfect spot to slow down and just enjoy island life.

Name: Pasikuda Beach
User_Review: Pasikudah Beach, located on Sri Lanka's East Coast, is famous for its calm, clear, and shallow waters, ideal for safe swimming, snorkeling, and water sports due to a protective offshore coral reef. The beach offers wide sandy stretches and a variety of resorts, making it a beautiful and tranquil destination for families and water sports enthusiasts.

Name: Arugam Bay Point Surf Spot
User_Review: Even if you not surfing or surfing good it's nice to sit and see others, doing it well

Name: Arugam Bay Point Surf Spot
User_Review: This is one of the famous places in Arugam bay. A surfing spot and a bathing spot among local and foreign travellers. It has shallow waters where you can enjoy your swim and a little bit further you will find the best waves for a nice surf. crowded in the afternoon. Morning is nice for a cool surf.

Name: Arugam Bay Point Surf Spot
User_Review: Awesome place 😍😍I really love this place.Some facilities are not available for locals 😣

Name: Arugam Bay Point Surf Spot
User_Review:
Swimming Best Spot Arugambay !!
Beautiful Beach.

Name: Arugam Bay Point Surf Spot
User_Review: The crowded surfing sessions I ever been in. The locals gives you no chance to surf quietly, and make sure that you gonna know their locals (they have zero style btw). If you a beginner and you want to take some lessons watch out from the local instructors (warning for the ladies) I often heard complains that they touch and growp students, it happen in the city also, they just can't leave their hands for themselves. Better to go surf in the points that are far from the city, but it's still an all around problem. I'll never go back there for sure.

Name: Lahugala National Park
User_Review:
Nestled away from the beaten path lies one of Sri Lanka's best-kept secrets, a national park that boasts tranquility and unparalleled wildlife sightings. Having had the privilege of visiting this hidden gem twice, I can attest to its allure and pristine beauty.

What sets this park apart is its blissful lack of traffic. On one occasion, only a single jeep ventured into the park for the entire month, a testament to its untouched wilderness. This rarity ensures an intimate wildlife experience, undisturbed by the crowds found in more popular destinations.

The discovery of sloth bear footprints further underscored the park's rich biodiversity, while herds of elephants roamed freely, offering glimpses of their majestic presence. Despite its proximity to the renowned Kumana National Park, this hidden gem boasts a unique habitat reminiscent of riverine forests. This distinct environment not only sets it apart but also provides a haven for diverse bird species. I was fortunate enough to spot the rare Greater Racket-tailed Drongo during my birdwatching excursions, adding to the park's allure for avian enthusiasts.

A highlight of exploration within the park is reaching the end of the main road, where one can disembark from the safari jeep and venture on foot along the riverbanks. This immersive experience allows for a deeper connection with nature and often leads to remarkable encounters with wildlife.

For travelers venturing to the southeastern reaches of Sri Lanka, this park is a must-visit destination. Whether you seek solitude amidst nature's embrace or yearn for thrilling wildlife encounters, this hidden gem promises an unforgettable journey through the heart of Sri Lanka's wilderness.

Name: Lahugala National Park
User_Review: This national park is surrounded by two water ways Heda oya and Karanda oya. It has 3 major water reservoirs named Lahugala Maha wewa, Kithulana wewa and Sengamuwa wewa. Most common faunal attractions are elephants, crocodiles and sloth bears. There’s a famous flora - a grass alled ‘Beru’, which is a favorite food of elephants.

Name: Lahugala National Park
User_Review: Amazingly beautiful. I only rode through on my bike on my way to Hiriketiya and l saw 6+ elephants, water buffalo, crocodiles, coyotes, monitor lizards, chital dear and so many birds it was crazy. I didn’t even need to go on a paid tour. Then l had to return on my way back to Colombo and l saw even more animals like wild pigs and Rudda mongoose

Name: Lahugala National Park
User_Review: Nice park with lots of animals, but not many tourists. Plenty of buffalos, wild pigs, deer, we also saw foxes, mongoose and an elephant. Myriad of birds, any bird lover will

Name: Lahugala National Park
User_Review: Popular for elephant sightings during dry season. Difficult to see much elephants during rainy season from Nov to Feb. Monaragala Pottuvil road crosses the national park. Elephants can be watched even from the main road during evening. Only one DWC circuit bunglow is available inside the park.

Name: Kudumbigala Monastery Cave
User_Review:
Kudumbigala Monastery is a remote but truly worthy place to visit. It’s a very sacred site, so it’s important to dress properly, behave silently, and show good manners when you come here. The atmosphere is so peaceful, and you really feel the spiritual energy of the monks and the surroundings.

The monastery is deep inside the jungle, and the road leading there is just a bumpy mud road. You can get through if you go slowly, but don’t expect nearby facilities. so bring whatever you need with you.

Climbing to the top of the rock is an amazing experience. The path is clear, with steps and railings that make it easier, though you still need a bit of fitness. I recommend starting around 6 am before it gets hot. The view from the top, along with the unique stupa, is absolutely worth it.

This is one of those places that’s not just about sightseeing, but about respecting and connecting with the sacred space. A visit here leaves you with peace of mind and a deep appreciation for history, nature, and spirituality

Name: Kudumbigala Monastery Cave
User_Review: Kudumbigala Monastery is so tranquil and serene. Tucked away in the jungle, The hike up the rock is a bit of a workout, but the jaw-dropping views of lush greenery and the ocean make it totally worth it. It’s super peaceful, perfect for soaking in some history and zen. Just be respectful—this place is sacred. A hidden gem for sure!

Name: Kudumbigala Monastery Cave
User_Review:
The Kudumbigala monastic complex is a significant historical and religious site in Sri Lanka, with a rich and tumultuous history. Established in 246 BC during the reign of King Devanampiyatissa, it served as a refuge for Buddhist monks seeking solitude away from bustling urban centers. The complex, comprising over 200 rock caves, is a testament to the early Buddhist monastic tradition in Sri Lanka. The discovery of inscriptions in Brahmi script and other archaeological evidence in the Maha Sudharshana Lena cave further underscores its ancient origins, linking it to the pre-Christian era and the patronage of figures like Nandimitra, one of King Dutugemunu’s ten giant warriors.

However, the monastery's history is also marked by tragedy and neglect. In 1994, the LTTE (Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam) carried out a brutal attack in the nearby village of Panama, killing 17 innocent civilians. This event forced the abandonment of Kudumbigala, as the government was unable to ensure its protection. The LTTE's actions, including desecrating the Buddha statue by throwing tar on its head, were deliberate attempts to intimidate the monks and erase the cultural and religious significance of the site.

The broader context of ethnic conflict in Sri Lanka, particularly the Tamil separatist movement, led to the displacement of Sinhalese Buddhists from large areas of the country. Despite the profound cultural and historical importance of sites like Kudumbigala, many Sinhalese Buddhists remain unaware of their heritage, and a smaller percentage of Christian Sinhalese seem indifferent to its preservation.

After years of abandonment due to the civil war, Kudumbigala stood as a silent witness to the erasure and rediscovery of Sri Lanka's rich cultural landscape. The monastery's unique cylindrical dagoba, the only one of its kind surviving in Sri Lanka, highlights its architectural and spiritual significance. The site's revival and protection are crucial for preserving the legacy of Sinhalese civilization and ensuring that such sacred places are not lost to history. The story of Kudumbigala is a poignant reminder of the resilience of cultural heritage in the face of conflict and the need for collective efforts to safeguard it for future generations.

Name: Kudumbigala Monastery Cave
User_Review:
For the past decade, my family and I have made an annual pilgrimage to the serene Kudumbigala Buddhist Monastery every December for alms giving. This sacred haven, nestled amidst breathtaking natural beauty and wildlife, serves as a sanctuary where devoted monks fervently practice Buddhism and meditation in pursuit of spiritual enlightenment.

Visitors are implored to approach this sacred space with the utmost respect, refraining from littering, shouting, or disturbing the monks and wildlife. Instead, immerse yourself in the tranquil environment, absorbing the rich tapestry of history, nature, and Buddhism that permeates every corner of this tranquil abode.

Given its remote location, I recommend organizing group visits and ensuring you're well-prepared with provisions, as there are no nearby shops. Let your journey to Kudumbigala be a soul-stirring experience, fostering reverence and appreciation for the profound teachings and natural splendor it offers. ☺️

Name: Kudumbigala Monastery Cave
User_Review: Kudumbigala Tapovanaya in Sri Lanka is a world-famous retreat for meditators. A haven of nature, this place is the birthplace of Buddhist monks in the past. Archaeological evidence has been found that suggests that the Arahants lived here in the past. This place, filled with nature and wildlife, is a haven for meditation.

Name: Ella Rock Viewpoint
User_Review: Beautiful views from the top, with Little Adam’s Peak visible on the horizon and several great viewpoints along the way. The hike takes about 40 minutes and is quite intense, but absolutely worth it. We went in the evening—unfortunately, you can’t see the sunset, so sunrise is probably the better option. Don’t forget to bring headlamps!

Name: Ella Rock Viewpoint
User_Review:
Nice hike and great view from the top, less crowded than Little Adams Peak.

I recommend 2 routes:

1) Walk on the train tracks towards Kithalella station. 110m before the station there is a arrow on the track (see picture below, orange color has mostly faded) and ella rock written next to it. There are stairs leading down next to some local's house and over a little bridge. It seems a bit sketchy but is actually the fastet route. From there on just follow the orange dots/ arrows.

2) This is the route you'll find most often online. Walk on the tracks 400m past Kithalella station and take a sharp left after the big rock (see picture). After crossing the bridge turn left right away upwards into the tea plantages (the path might be blocked by cut down trees, ignore these and walk up anyway). From here on stay on that path until you arrive at orange dots/ arrows, which you can follow to the top. This path takes around 15-20m longer than the first one.

Keep in mind: Don't listen to the locals, they will trick you into taking different routes where only they know the way (for their advice you would need to pay...)

Name: Ella Rock Viewpoint
User_Review:
Hiking to Ella Rock is one of the unique experiences you can have in Ella. The scenery is breathtaking. The hike starts about 3 km from Kithal Ella Railway Station and takes around 1 to 1.5 hours to reach the top. The final 1 km is the most challenging part, with a steep incline.
Overall, it was a great experience. Note that you need to buy entry tickets at the top of the mountain.

Name: Ella Rock Viewpoint
User_Review: A must do if in Ella, highly recommend for sunrise: best view and you can beat the heat. Leave at 430a from nearby train station. Use AllTrails GPS if you prefer over guide. Trail is a bit rough in places, but a great challenge and exciting journey in the dark. Fee is 950 LKR at the top. Given the crowds I’ve seen so far in Ella, this was serene and refreshing: there were only around 20 people.

Name: Ella Rock Viewpoint
User_Review: One of the favourite hikes EVER - we started from Ella town and it took us just under 4 hours. Highly recommend following All Trails route and was nearly 12km in total. We hiked as two females and felt absolutely fine the whole way, just keep a listen out for trains when on the track 🤣, when on the rock it can get very overgrown and steep so I do recommend proper walking shoes. We also got a coconut from the base of the rock on the way back down which was a lovely refreshment. Worth noting that on our walk down we were approached to pay an entry/exit fee which was strange as we didn’t see any notice of this during the hike or when at the top - the staff also couldn’t confirm to us what the money was going towards

Name: Little Adam’s Peak
User_Review: Well this place is heaven. You have to walk up the mountains for a while you will question yourself why, but once you are right on top the place is to die for. Just mountains all around. So peaceful and so beautiful. The freshness in the air all the amazing view from the mountain top is the most beautiful experience. Definitely would love to travel here again just for this.

Name: Little Adam’s Peak
User_Review: Beautiful trial, easy to follow signs, arrived at a point where they were selling the experiences such as zipline or a ‘more scenic’ view which has you walk in a sort of net above the regular path, looked uncomfortable and short. The sign for ‘regular path’ is what we took which was excellent. It is mainly man made steps so very easy to follow, nearly impossible to go off track. Breathtaking views from the top!

Name: Little Adam’s Peak
User_Review:
Little Adam’s Peak is one of the must-do hikes in Ella. The trail is relatively easy and well-marked, making it accessible even for beginners. From the lower entrance, it takes about 15–20 minutes to reach the top at a steady pace.

Once you reach the peak, you’re rewarded with stunning panoramic views of the surrounding hills and valleys. It’s especially beautiful during sunrise or sunset. A great short hike that offers incredible scenery without too much effort.

Name: Little Adam’s Peak
User_Review: Amazing view and very quick hike. We did it for sunrise and it was excellent!

Name: Little Adam’s Peak
User_Review: We’re a Sri Lankan family, and hiking Little Adam’s Peak was one of the highlights of our Ella trip! The trail is well-marked, easy to follow, and family-friendly even our 10-year-old daughter loved the climb. The views from the top are absolutely breathtaking, especially in the morning light. Along the way, we enjoyed the fresh air, greenery, and peaceful vibe. It’s the perfect short hike for families who want a bit of adventure without it being too tough. Highly recommended!

Name: Diyaluma Falls Base Point
User_Review: Upper Diyaluma Waterfall is stunning and definitely worth the hike. The natural infinity pools at the top are a hidden gem. You can swim while enjoying panoramic views of the valley below. The trail is moderate and scenic, with plenty of chances for photos along the way. I recommend going early in the morning to avoid the heat and crowds. Also, wear good shoes and bring water. This was one of the highlights of my trip to Sri Lanka; it was peaceful, unspoiled, and truly magical. I highly recommend it for nature lovers and adventure seekers.

Name: Diyaluma Falls Base Point
User_Review:
First we saw Sri Lanka’s second tallest waterfall from the road, and after a moment of taking in, we considered moving on with our day. Thankfully we decided to drive about five minutes from here to the high view point car park (you can find this on the map) - which I highly recommend. We rented a scooter for the day for 2500 rupees, which was great as this area is an incredibly scenic drive. There was free parking by some shops and the shopkeepers are all very friendly. Some local guides offer tours (at cost) up to the viewing point but we decided to do the hike on our own. It took about half an hour.
When you get close to the top, there are two paths you can go. The right path takes you to the top of the incredibly tall waterfall you can see from the road. The views here are phenomenal. There are a few small ponds if you fancy a dip, although very small. This part is more for the amazing views. Give yourself some time to sit here and take it all in.
If you take the left path you can hike up further to the top of the back falls (pictured) which have a larger swim area at the top. Because we took the right path, we walked over a few rocks back from the view point to the bottom of these falls. This part is very beautiful and would be great to climb if you bring your togs. The top of these falls might be the best part if you want to submerge.
We went later in the day, and I would recommend setting aside 2-3 hours to relax here once you’ve made the commute.

There is also a fee of 350 rupees to enter.

Name: Diyaluma Falls Base Point
User_Review:
This is the best place to rest. The waterfall has cool water. Can swimming here there are many places like pools. Have some slides. Jumping points. So this place is wonderful. Also the view is amazing. Most tourists are coming to this place from Ella. Daily visitors are coming to see this beautiful place.

You have to buy tickets for entry to this beautiful place. They provide free toilets here. And have some steps provided for hillside access. So buying tickets is not useless.

Name: Diyaluma Falls Base Point
User_Review: The hike is moderate and manageable. I do not see any need of taking a guide. Just follow the Google Maps, but I think it tuktuk is necessary to go there, the tuk tuk will drop you off and you can hike on your own without any guide. This is the best place in Ella

Name: Diyaluma Falls Base Point
User_Review:
Amazing experience.. better to go in morning ..

Name: Rawana Falls Scenic Stop
User_Review: A beautiful and powerful waterfall located right off the main road — very easy to access. The view is impressive, especially during the rainy season when the water flow is strong. It can get quite crowded with tourists and vendors, and there’s limited space to relax near the falls. Swimming is not advised due to safety concerns, but it's still a great stop for photos and enjoying nature. Worth a visit if you’re in the Ella area!

Name: Rawana Falls Scenic Stop
User_Review: Ravana Falls is a beautiful stop, especially surrounded by the green scenery. We visited while it was raining, which made the experience different and fun, even if we ended up completely wet. The waterfall is impressive and worth a quick visit if you are in the area.

Name: Rawana Falls Scenic Stop
User_Review:
Ravana Waterfall – A Must-Visit Natural Wonder in Sri Lanka

Ravana Waterfall is one of the most beautiful and iconic waterfalls in Sri Lanka. We visited recently, and it was an unforgettable experience. The sight of the powerful water cascading down the rocks surrounded by lush greenery is absolutely breathtaking.

It’s easy to reach, located just a short drive from Ella, and a popular stop for both local and international travelers. The sound of the water and the cool mist in the air create a refreshing and peaceful atmosphere.

We truly enjoyed our visit and spent some time just relaxing and taking in the natural beauty. It’s a great spot for photos and a quick break if you’re exploring the Ella area.

Highly recommended for nature lovers and anyone looking to experience the stunning beauty of Sri Lanka!

Name: Rawana Falls Scenic Stop
User_Review:
Ravana Ella Waterfall. Sounds epic, right? Like something out of Lord of the Rings, with elves singing and Gandalf doing backflips. Reality check? It’s more like: tiny splash of water, massive splash of tourists. Seriously, there are more selfie sticks than actual drops falling.

Don’t get me wrong, it’s nice. Water’s falling, gravity’s doing its job, nature hasn’t gone on strike yet. But spectacular? Nah. Niagara is laughing somewhere. This one is more like “cute screensaver” level.

Verdict: worth a quick stop, maybe grab a photo to prove you were there, then escape before another busload of people elbows you out of the way.

Name: Rawana Falls Scenic Stop
User_Review: Beautiful waterflow. On the way to upcountry, you can have a visit on Ravana Ella. Its a beautiful waterfall  and its can see by the road. But, be aware of Monkeys🐒 there, don’t keep food,bags or something that it can see with you 😆 you cannot get it back.

Name: Lipton Seat Viewpoint
User_Review:
Must-Visit in Haputale!
Lipton’s Seat is one of the most breathtaking viewpoints in Sri Lanka. From the top, you get panoramic views of endless tea plantations, misty valleys, and mountains stretching far into the distance. Sunrise here is magical—arrive early before the clouds roll in. The journey through the tea estates is as beautiful as the destination itself. A peaceful, refreshing spot full of history and natural beauty. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Haputale!

Name: Lipton Seat Viewpoint
User_Review:
Lipton Seat is absolutely beautiful and offers an amazing view! I visited from Haputale town for the first time, and the road to get there is one of the most scenic routes I’ve ever traveled.

The road has steep climbs, sharp 180-degree bends, and is quite narrow in many places. There are a few small spots where vehicles can pass each other, so you need to be a skilled and patient driver. Some three-wheeler drivers will tell you that the road gets too narrow about 7–8 km from the top and try to convince you to take a tuk-tuk—but if you trust your driving, you can go all the way yourself.

I drove a 2017 Panda Cross and had no issues. Even smaller cars like the Wagon R can make it to the top. Only the last 1–1.5 km is a bit rough with some bumpy, stone-covered patches, but it’s still manageable with care.

The road winds through beautiful tea gardens and has a calm, peaceful vibe. Some people choose to walk, which is also a great option if you have time. There’s a real sense of serenity here—please help protect the environment while you enjoy it.

Definitely worth a visit if you’re traveling to Haputale

Name: Lipton Seat Viewpoint
User_Review: Beautiful place. The road is very scenic and one of a kind of experience. I personally advise to park the vehicle outside of the ticket area and walk to  lipton’s seat. It’s no more than about 1.3Km and with the views and the trees you wouldn’t even feel the distance. The canteen there is a total ripoff do not buy from there. They over charge you.

Name: Lipton Seat Viewpoint
User_Review:
This was my second visit to Lipton’s Seat. The road condition has improved compared to before, though there are still some potholes along the way. I took the route from Haputale town — the road is very narrow all the way to the peak, and you need to be a somewhat experienced driver to manage it. Many times, either I or the other driver had to reverse the vehicle to a wider spot to give way, which can be quite tricky.

When we arrived, the entire place was covered in mist. We waited for about an hour until it cleared and were rewarded with breathtaking views all around.

Some of the photos I’ve shared here are from the Haputale–Lipton’s Seat route, which is just as beautiful as the summit itself. The drive is surrounded by lush tea plantations and historic bungalows from the colonial era, making the journey as memorable as the destination.

Name: Lipton Seat Viewpoint
User_Review: Lipton’s Seat is one of the most breathtaking viewpoints in Sri Lanka, located in the highlands of Haputale. Surrounded by endless tea plantations, it offers stunning panoramic views of several provinces on a clear day. This peaceful spot, where Sir Thomas Lipton once sat to survey his tea empire, is perfect for nature lovers and those seeking a quiet escape in the hills. A truly iconic and refreshing experience in Sri Lanka’s tea country.

Name: Demodara Loop Railway
User_Review:
A must-visit spot if you’re in Ella or Demodara! Only the last part of the road is a bit rough, but we took a tuk-tuk from Demodara Station (both ways cost 2500 LKR), which made the trip easy. The ride is about 20 minutes with no need for walking, as the tuk-tuk can go all the way up to the top.

At the viewpoint, there’s a small café where you can relax with a king coconut, tea, or some snacks while enjoying the stunning view of the famous Demodara Railway Loop. From here, you can see around 10 km of the train’s journey, with breathtaking scenery all around. Watching the train curve around the loop from above is truly worth the trip. A unique and memorable experience!

Name: Demodara Loop Railway
User_Review: Awesome view. You think go there to see the train. Please look before the train time table.

Name: Demodara Loop Railway
User_Review: This is the best spot to witness the iconic Demodara train loop. You can see around 10km of track from here with an almost 360-degree view. The road is narrow and not well-maintained, but accessible by tuk-tuk for around Rs. 2500  but worth it. Don't miss this if you visit Demodara.

Name: Demodara Loop Railway
User_Review: Great view point. Don’t miss this chance. We found 4x4 team in Demodara railway station. For up and down they charge Rs 5,500 per vehicle and for 10 people. Road are not much difficult. But don’t go with low ground clearance vehicle. Very clear view. In there you can see railway loop, Punagala mountain range, Ella rock, Little adams peak and Namunukuna mountain. Make sure to check train operating time before go. Then you can view the this loop works..

Name: Demodara Loop Railway
User_Review: I had the opportunity to view a train crossing Demodara loop. Tuk tuk may charge Rs.2500+ to climb to the top. Road is narrow & not well maintained. Name: Namunukula Mountain Range
User_Review:
The peak of Namunukula mountain is the best destination for camping. Very beautiful view. Highly recommended for camping. Cold climate. Little bit hard hike. this is the 12th highest mountain in Sri Lanka.

Evening view and morning view absolutely beautiful. It's better to start a hike in the morning time.

Namunukula is one of the most popular hiking spots in Sri Lanka. It is best hiked during the drier months as the lesser rainfall also provides for lesser leeches. You will also have to take plenty of water as there are no clean water sources as you make the climb. The summit of the tallest peak of the mountain range can be reached by several trails.

Name: Namunukula Mountain Range
User_Review:
The name “Namunukula” means nine peaks which depicts the peaks of this mountain range. The Namunukula Mountain Range consists of the tallest mountain peak in the Uva Province standing at 2,035 meters which is also the 12th tallest mountain in Sri Lanka.

The Namunukula Mountain Range is a popular hiking spot in the Uva Province and is best hiked during the dry months as there is less rainfall resulting in lesser leeches. Leech protection is required in the form of anti-leech socks which is suitable since the anti-leech spray could easily wash off from skin from rain water.

The summit of the tallest peak of the Namunukula Mountain Range can be reached by several trails. The best and easiest route starts off at the 3rd Mile Post on the Passara-Badulla Road. It can be travelled by vehicle until the base of the mountain in the Deyangalla division in Glenalpin Estate.

Name: Namunukula Mountain Range
User_Review:
Namunukula Mountain Range is a mountain range situated in the Uva Province of Sri Lanka. The name “Namunukula” means nine peaks which depicts the peaks of this mountain range. The Namunukula Mountain Range consists of the tallest mountain peak in the Uva Province standing at 2,035 meters which is also the 12th tallest mountain in Sri Lanka.

The Namunukula Mountain Range is a popular hiking spot in the Uva Province and is best hiked during the dry months as there is less rainfall resulting in lesser leeches. Leech protection is required in the form of anti-leech socks which is suitable since the anti-leech spray could easily wash off from skin from rain water.

Getting to Namunukula Mountain Range

From Ella take the Ella – Passara Road and go past Balleketuwa and Passara up until you reach Namunukula.

The summit of the tallest peak of the Namunukula Mountain Range can be reached by several trails. The best and easiest route starts off at the 3rd Mile Post on the Passara-Badulla Road. It can be travelled by vehicle until the base of the mountain in the Deyangalla division in Glenalpin Estate.

Name: Buduruwagala Ancient Stone Carvings
User_Review: Just amazing! It’s not clear when was this sculpted. but it could be from King Walagambha era. It’s depicting Mahayana Buddhism. in the middle you see Sri Gauthama Samma Sam Buddha. on to ur right there are three future Bodhisathwa who will become future Buddhas. on to the left it’s the Avalokitheshwara Bodisathwa, Thara Devi and their son “Sudana”. Wish the relevant government department would place a hut to protect this valuable sculptures as the paints used thousands of years ago is still visible and the workmanship is  exquisite!

Name: Buduruwagala Ancient Stone Carvings
User_Review: The abundance and preservation of archaeological sites here are truly remarkable! It feels like stepping into a living museum—every corner has a story to tell, and history is beautifully intact. Nothing compares to the perfect blend of history and nature. This place is serene and sacred at the same time. Though I am not a Buddhist, but witnessing this real life carvings are truly beyond words.

Name: Buduruwagala Ancient Stone Carvings
User_Review:
🌿 A Mind-Blowing Experience at Buduruwagala Temple 🇱🇰 🌿

Visiting Buduruwagala was truly an unforgettable and awe-inspiring experience. Hidden deep within a peaceful forest, this ancient site features magnificent rock carvings that speak volumes about the incredible craftsmanship and spiritual depth of Sri Lanka’s ancient civilization.

The massive standing Buddha and surrounding figures carved directly into the rock face are absolutely breathtaking — both in size and detail. It’s mind-blowing to think these masterpieces were created over a thousand years ago, with such precision and grace, using only simple tools.

The calm and serene environment adds to the spiritual atmosphere, making it a perfect place for reflection and admiration. If you love history, nature, or are simply in search of something extraordinary, Buduruwagala is a must-visit.

✨ A true hidden gem that captures the soul of Sri Lanka’s heritage. Don’t miss it! ✨

Name: Buduruwagala Ancient Stone Carvings
User_Review: An interesting visit to an ancient monastery that we happened to come across on our travel to Ella from Matara. The monastery dates back to the 10thC and is considered to be the highest standing Buddha Statue in the world and is said to be an interesting example of the Mahayana tradition found.on.the island.

Name: Madulsima Mini Worlds End
User_Review:
Mini World’s End in Madolsima is a breathtaking viewpoint that truly lives up to its name. Perched high in the hills of the Uva Province, this hidden gem offers sweeping views of lush valleys, misty mountains, and tea plantations that seem to stretch endlessly into the horizon. On clear mornings, you can even see the distant outlines of the eastern plains — a sight that leaves you in quiet awe.

The journey to Mini World’s End is part of the adventure. The road winds through picturesque tea estates about a killometer, giving a glimpse of rural Sri Lankan life. The final stretch involves a short hike through cool, mist-laden paths, making the experience even more rewarding for nature lovers and photographers. (Difficulty level ~mild no wheelchair access)

It’s generally less crowded than the more famous World’s End in Horton Plains, which makes it peaceful and ideal for those who prefer serenity over tourist hustle. However, the cliff edge is steep, so caution is essential, especially if visiting with children.

The best time to visit is early morning, before the mist rolls in and hides the view. Bring a jacket — the air can be quite chilly — and don’t forget your camera to capture the stunning sunrise over the valleys below.

Overall, Mini World’s End, Madolsima, is a tranquil and scenic spot that perfectly showcases the untouched beauty of Sri Lanka’s hill country — a must-visit for travelers seeking natural charm, solitude, and unforgettable views.

Beware of wasps!!

Name: Madulsima Mini Worlds End
User_Review:
Madolsima Mini World's End in the Cocogalla Estate, Pitamaruwa, 40 kms from Badulla, is another breathtaking destination, with an elevation of 3,400 feet at its highest point. The view point offers stunning views of the greenery and scenic beauty of surrounding landscape and mountains.
You need to walk through a scenic 1.5 km trail from the drop off point to Mini World's End view point.
It's best to visit the point early morning as you can soak in the beauty of the landscape in the cool of the morning and chilly winds blowing in your face.
As the day gets warmer there's also a risk of bees and hornets buzzing around and if you are unlucky there's a possibility of getting stung.
In wet weather beware of leeches.

Name: Madulsima Mini Worlds End

Within the past week, about 10 people have been attacked by hornets. We were also attacked by them. Two of our friends were stuck in the tent, and we had to run about 3 kilometers. I was stung by three hornets myself. Based on my experience, I would not recommend going there.

Name: Dondra Lighthouse
User_Review:
Dondra Head, also known as Devinuwara, marks the southernmost point of Sri Lanka and is a place of both natural beauty and cultural significance. It’s most famous for the Dondra Head Lighthouse, an impressive white octagonal tower that rises 49 meters above sea level and stands as one of the tallest lighthouses in South Asia. Surrounded by swaying coconut palms and the deep blue waters of the Indian Ocean, the site offers spectacular coastal views and a serene, breezy atmosphere that instantly calms visitors. On clear days, the panoramic view from the lighthouse grounds is breathtaking — the endless stretch of ocean creates a perfect spot for photography and quiet reflection.

The area around Dondra is peaceful and less commercialized than other coastal attractions, allowing visitors to experience authentic southern coastal life. A small Buddhist temple and local fishing communities add cultural charm, while the rocky shoreline and crashing waves make it an excellent place for a scenic walk or picnic. It’s also one of the best places in Sri Lanka for whale watching, especially between November and April, when blue whales and dolphins are often spotted just offshore.

Though visitors are not always allowed to climb to the top of the lighthouse, the site itself remains well worth a stop. Its combination of history, ocean views, and tranquil surroundings make Dondra Head a hidden gem for travelers exploring the Matara area.

Name: Dondra Lighthouse
Review_Author: DHANANJAYA DIAS / ධනංජය ඩයස් / தனஞ்சய டயஸ்
User_Review:
I visited Dondra Head in Deundara with my family, and it was an unforgettable experience. This place marks the southernmost tip of Sri Lanka, where the land meets the endless Indian Ocean. The main attraction is the historic Dondra Head Lighthouse – one of the tallest in South Asia – which stands proudly by the coastline. Even from outside, the lighthouse looks magnificent and makes the perfect backdrop for photos.

The area around Dondra Head is peaceful and beautifully maintained. The rocky shore, combined with the strong sea breeze and sound of the waves, creates a truly calming atmosphere. We spent time walking around, enjoying the views, and capturing some amazing pictures together. It’s also an excellent spot if you want to relax away from crowds or witness a stunning sunrise or sunset over the ocean.

Reaching the location was easy, as the roads are well-connected from Matara. Overall, our family really enjoyed the visit. Dondra Head is a perfect blend of history, natural beauty, and serenity. Highly recommend it for anyone traveling through the southern coast of Sri Lanka.

Name: Dondra Lighthouse
User_Review:
🌊 Dondra Head – Where Sri Lanka Meets the Edge of the World

Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐☆ (4/5)

Dondra Head is a peaceful, picturesque location that marks the southernmost tip of Sri Lanka. Located just a short drive from Matara, this spot offers a perfect blend of natural beauty, coastal charm, and cultural significance.

🗺️ What Makes It Special

Standing at Dondra Head gives you the surreal feeling of being at the very end of the island. The views of the endless Indian Ocean are breathtaking—especially when waves crash against the rocky shoreline. It’s a great spot to unwind, reflect, and take in the untouched beauty of southern Sri Lanka.

The area is also home to Dondra Head Lighthouse, one of the tallest in South Asia. Though entry into the lighthouse itself is not always permitted, just being near it is impressive. The white structure rising above the palm trees with the ocean as a backdrop makes for incredible photo ops.

⸻

🙏 Local Culture & Spirituality

Nearby is the Sri Vishnu Maha Devalaya, a sacred Hindu temple complex that’s both historic and architecturally intriguing. If you’re interested in cultural exploration, it’s worth a short visit before or after the lighthouse.

⸻

🚶‍♂️Things to Do
•	Walk along the coast and enjoy the sound of crashing waves and the breeze.
•	Photography: Ideal for coastal landscape photography and lighthouse backdrops.
•	Watch local fishermen: Often seen casting nets or pulling boats ashore—authentic everyday life in the south.
•	Enjoy the peace: Unlike more touristy spots, Dondra is relatively quiet and peaceful, especially on weekdays.

⸻

🍴 Food & Facilities

There aren’t many restaurants or shops right at the point, so it’s best to bring water or snacks with you. However, the town of Dondra and nearby Matara have plenty of cafes and eateries for a proper meal before or after your visit.

⸻

🧭 Travel Tips
•	Best time to visit: Morning or late afternoon to avoid harsh sunlight.
•	Wear comfortable shoes: The rocks near the shore can be uneven and slippery.
•	Transport: You can reach Dondra by tuk-tuk, private car, or even train to Matara, followed by a short ride.
•	Photography tip: Sunset here is beautiful, with golden light hitting the lighthouse and ocean.

⸻

📝 Final Thoughts

Dondra Head may not be packed with activities, but its charm lies in the serenity and symbolism of being at the edge of the island. It’s perfect for a short peaceful stop, especially for travelers exploring the southern coast. Great for photography lovers, culture seekers, and anyone who appreciates nature away from the crowds.

The kite trips were really well organized, the conditions were amazing and I felt safe and taken care of at all times. Fathy was a very talented and caring instructor and even once my course was finished he still supported me with the set-up of my equipment and gave me tips on how I can improve. 10/10 recommend and will be back once I get the chance :)

Name: Kitesurf Kalpitiya Lagoon
User_Review: Beautiful location surrounded by nature with very comfortable beds and air conditioning. The staff was very kind and accommodating and the kite surfing location/ instructors were excellent! Food was tasty, varied and always enough :) For solo travelers I would recommend checking the weather forecast before going as the camp empties out on low wind days.

Name: Kitesurf Kalpitiya Lagoon
User_Review:
My first kitesurf experience of my life and it was perfect.

The teacher are very good, the resort is very confortable, and the food is on point every night. Without mentioning the personnel who is always here to help.

100% recommend !

Name: Kitesurf Kalpitiya Lagoon
User_Review: One of the best places we have been to. Very friendly staff, great food (full board) and affordable kite surf packages. They really go the extra mile and offer a variety of activities. Yoga, beach volleyball, gym sessions, parties etc. It should be clear that this cannot be done with super low prices. The best part for us was the  kite surfing of course. Teachers, service on the beach and different excursions to nearby location for more advanced riders. We levelled up from barely riding in one direction to master even a few jumps. We will come again.

Name: Muthurajawela Wetland Boat Tour
User_Review: Great boat ride with Praneeth. Very skilled and knowledgeable about local birds and wildlife. Super friendly and happy. We had seasonal fruit on the boat and he stopped in certain places to take in the environment which was very good. Highly recommended!

Name: Muthurajawela Wetland Boat Tour
User_Review: Lovely day on a boat. Tour guide was an amazing guy, shared some fruits with us and was very welcoming. Showed us the birds and we got to see some monkeys

Name: Muthurajawela Wetland Boat Tour
User_Review: We had a fantastic morning on the water with Praneeth, who was an EXCELLENT guide. His knowledge about the local mangroves and wildlife, especially the birds, was very impressive. We highly recommend this tour and look forward to being back!

Name: Muthurajawela Wetland Boat Tour
User_Review:
Great tour on the Muthurajawela lagoon and wetlands by our super-friendly, enthousiastic, knowledgeable and skilled guide Praneeth.
After a warm welcome and introduction we went for a 2+ hour trip along the mangrove forest and wetlands. We've spotted several types of kingfishers,  cormorran, herron, a big lizzard and even some monkeys. We were very well cared for with bananas,  melons and coconuts and Praneeth is a joy and a gift as a guide.  Highly recommended!

Name: Muthurajawela Wetland Boat Tour
User_Review: Great first day in Sri Lanka Negombo nature lagoon boat ride.  Our guide was absolutely delightful.   Very knowledgeable on birds and plants.  He even brought home grown bananas and coconuts for treats.  So glad we made the trip from USA.

Name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary
User_Review: It's a quaint place on a quiet beach. Our cabana was set in a huge compound area all to ourselves with an outdoor shower and an outdoor bathroom area. The king size  bed had mosquito nets. The pool faces the sea and is long and well kept. The Sri Lankan food is good but do not try the western style dishes. The staff is friendly without being intrusive. You will be taken to see hundreds of spinner dolphins but check that the boat allocated to you has a well maintained engine.  Ours left us stranded but a replacement boat was sent to rescue us reasonably quickly.  If you like quiet places with very few people these cabanas on this peaceful beach are definitely for you.

Name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary
User_Review:
It is a beautiful place with some minor flaws. Food is okay, not good. It took the waiters about 15 min to bring salt and pepper, at every meal. And we asked for it every time. If you order a la card, the food is never hot. Its warm, almost cold.
Breakfast is ok, Coffee is not good. I had better Breakfast in places that cost much much less. But still, the resort, the beach the pool, the lighting is very nice, so we had a good time.

Name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary
User_Review: A very nice resort directly on the beach. We spent 4 nights in the resort, the villa was perfect for our family (including 1 kid and 2 teenagers). To take my shower in the garden of the private villa was a pleasure. The staff is particularly friendly and helpful, the gardens are perfectly maintained, the pool is amazing. The meals are made homemade with fresh vegetables and fruits. And above all do not forget to ask the manager Sameera to take you on a safari to the park of Wilpattu : sometimes getting up before dawn makes you discover extraordinary places...

Name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary
User_Review:
One of my favourite places in the world! Understated back-to-nature luxury, genuinely wonderful staff, and the pool...oh the pool was divine! I'd have a wedding there, honey moon, parties, you name it, it works!

It's remote though so great for a retreaty style. The local town is accessible but small

Name: Bar Reef Marine Sanctuary
User_Review: Very nice hotel, nice food. Very different atmosphere between the air conditioned rooms (on the pictures) and the other bungalows, situated among small gardens, more like a Robinson Crusoe experience. In the windy season no need for the air conditioned. Nice food too.very calm, not many things to see outside the hotel.
